data.name: v0.33.0
data.body:
# What's Changed

This release enhances the transaction management experience with improvements to file content viewing, active credential visibility, transaction snapshotting for account and node keys, and execution timestamp tracking. It also includes several stability fixes covering signature validation, special file encoding, keychain authentication, and reactive file content handling.

## Features

* feat: add executedAt timestamp to transaction status updates by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2975
* feat: display file contents in modal instead of saving to disk by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3004
* feat: enhance FileContentsModal UX by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3009
* feat: show active credentials for personal and organization accounts by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3007
* feat: implement transaction snapshotting for account and node keys  by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2984
* feat: email notifications are now disabled by default by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3019
* feat: update workflow cache removal and release automation docs to reflect current process by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3018

## Fixes

* fix: use computedAsync to handle fileID/file contents changes by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3005
* fix: validate signatures before recording in POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/3006
* fix: encode special file content before submission for Hedera special file IDs by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2988
* fix: remove GET /transaction-groups endpoint by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgrap

data.name: v0.32.0
data.body:
# What's Changed
Version 0.32.0 focuses on UI/UX improvements and expanded transaction group functionality, including a visual upgrade for transaction groups, a new "sign all & next" workflow that targets only unsigned transactions, and enhanced forms for Registered Node creation and editing. The release also addresses several reliability fixes such as graceful macOS Keychain denial recovery, corrected email notification logic, and enforced SSL in database connections, alongside automated release artifact signing.

## Features

* feat: Create transaction group visual upgrade by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2881
* feat: improve registered node Description input by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2909
* feat: improving service endpoint layout in Registered Node create/update form by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2910
* feat: improving user feedback of Payer ID field in Draft creation by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2905
* feat: provide sign all&next functionality to the transaction groups by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2911
* feat: enforce SSL in TypeORM configuration for SQL connection by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2918
* feat: re-worked endpoint editing in Registered Node Create/Update by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2913
* feat: add cleanup step to delete build artifacts cache after tests by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2916
* feat: increase transaction name length and enhance error logging by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2939
* feat: update sign all functionality to only process unsigned transactions by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pu

data.name: v0.31.0
data.body:
# What's Changed
This release adds full support for HIP-1137 registered node transactions (create, update, and delete) across both front-end and back-end, along with non-organizational signature support and a visual upgrade to the transaction group creation flow. It also ships several bug fixes around duplicate transaction ID handling, key signing filters, modal behavior, and group valid start updates.

## Features

* feat: details page for Token Transfer now displays all transfer types by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2854
* feat: allow keys in Public Keys to be used in Account transactions by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2857
* feat: hip-1137 create registered node by @Kolpic in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2853
* feat: HIP 1137 support associated registered nodes by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2874
* feat: added support for RegisteredNodeDeleteTransaction to POST /transactions by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2884
* feat: adding support for RegisteredNodeDeleteTransaction to front-end by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2886
* feat: adding backend support for RegisteredNodeUpdate transactions by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2891
* feat: non organizational signature support by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2896
* feat: adding front-end support for registered node update transactions by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2892
* feat: create transaction group visual upgrade (#2881) by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2903

## Fixes

* fix: inconsistent visibility of save complex key by @Kolpic in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-

data.name: v0.30.0
data.body:
# What's Changed
This release introduces improved release automation, restored and enhanced test coverage, and workspace optimizations, along with stronger transaction handling and notification reliability. It also delivers numerous bug fixes, better cache and state management, and dependency updates to improve overall stability and performance.

## Features

* feat: add release automation workflow  by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2776
* feat: restore regression tests by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2783
* feat: use pnpm workspaces by @Kolpic in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2779

## Fixes

* fix: update test scenarios for accuracy and clarity by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2763
* fix: restore organization notification automation suite by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2777
* fix: cannot set threshold key as file key by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2780
* fix: make sure account balance is refreshed after transaction execution by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2784
* fix: unread badge doesn't support 3+ digits by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2795
* fix: enable migration and allow for proper version checking flows by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2794
* fix: refactoring tooltip management by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2798
* fix: enable wait on first connect for NATS JetStream service by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2801
* fix: ensure addNodeKeys is called for node ID zero by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2802
* fix: app password leave dialog flow in a stale empty state

# What's Changed

This release focuses on infrastructure improvements including cache grouping and preload enablement, along with expanded test coverage across frontend unit and automation suites. Several bug fixes address transaction signing edge cases, file hash display, and flaky CI tests.

## Features

* feat: increase number of workers in CI by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2716

data.name: v0.28.0
data.body:
# What's Changed

This release brings several user-facing improvements including better error reporting, clearer UI interactions, and enhanced transaction handling. It also includes significant infrastructure work around parallel E2E test execution, NATS connection resilience, and a large batch of dependency updates. Bugs found during hybrid signing have also been addressed.

## Features
* feat: bump frontend and backend versions to 0.28.0-SNAPSHOT by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2584
* feat: clarify modal button labels by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2597
* feat: Sign All now provides better error reporting by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2595
* feat: enable shared parallel frontend E2E execution with single Solo/back-end setup by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2577
* feat: automation hooks and attach-mode state handling on top of shared parallel E2E execution by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2582
* feat: update size checks and smart collation for hip-1300 transactions by @Kolpic in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2608
* feat: make sure dependabot tracks dependencies for all components by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2615
* feat: restore org recovery coverage and stabilize E2E suites by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2706
* feat: improve NATS connection resilience and error handling by @Kolpic in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2526

## Fixes
* fix: AppConfirmModel sometimes forgets to call cancel() callback by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2589
* fix: TransactionBaseModel.computeSignatureKey() must propagate exceptions by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/

## Features
* feat: harden mirror node polling by @yasenltd in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2509

## Fixes

data.name: v0.26.0
data.body:
# What's Changed

This release streamlines group signing flows, improves notification reliability and control, and hardens node and public-key handling to avoid confusing failures. It also refines several UI details (toasts, modals, hidden features) and updates CI/CD and dependency tooling to keep the project secure and maintainable.

## Features

* feat: set `sign next` as the default action in signing process by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2360  
* feat: add configuration for dev app updates with release URL by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2392  
* feat: collapsing duplicate or overcount error messages by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2446  
* feat: disable external badges on transactions based on feature flag by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2482  
* feat: add missing notification opt-out options for transaction evens by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2484  
* feat: make Sign All action modal by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2485  

## Fixes

* fix: Organization tests fixes by @Yotsev in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2362  
* fix: update installation instructions for automatic updates by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2391  
* fix: improve user-facing error messages when fetching transactions by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2397  
* fix: handle case when creator key is deleted in transaction processing by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2381  
* fix: certain email types not displaying subject by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2405  
* fix: Improve cache refresh error handling for offline mirror nodes by @ddeskov-limecha

data.name: v0.25.0
data.body:
# What's Changed
This release delivers multiple UX improvements to transaction navigation and signing, better visibility into transaction and client status, and a wide range of stability, performance, and automation enhancements across the app. It also includes numerous dependency updates, test improvements, and fixes for edge-case behaviors discovered in real usage.

## Features
* feat: redesigned logic for Prev/Next flow by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2190
* feat: added Settings menu item and fix other items in Application menu  by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2199
* feat: improve UI of Previous/Next buttons by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2198
* feat: allow to sign and go to next transaction in a single click by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2214
* feat: more informative titles for transaction/group details pages by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2222
* feat: enhance automated update handling and improve conflict modal by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2225
* feat: Add expiring soon status by @ddeskov-limechain in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2196
* feat: Showing the freeze type on the transaction list table by @ddeskov-limechain in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2170
* feat: Endpoint Input auto strips protocol by @ddeskov-limechain in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2171
* feat: move badges to the transaction status section by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2292
* feat: enabling skipping Account Setup wizard in Personal mode by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2287
* feat: add breadcrumb to transaction/group details pages by @svienot in htt

data.name: v0.25.0-beta.1
data.body:
This beta release streamlines transaction review and signing with redesigned Previous/Next navigation, a new "sign & next" one-click flow, and clearer details via breadcrumbs, better titles, and repositioned badges. It also adds "expiring soon" status, preserves pagination/sorting across navigation, refines update handling and notifications (including signing events marking items as read), and delivers stability through key fixes like correct tab/history display, key caching for signing, and macOS app closure.

**Please test:**  
- Previous/Next and "sign & next" workflows with multiple transactions  
- Notification clearing during rapid signing/navigation  
- Pagination persistence and "expiring soon" status display  
- Automated updates and app closure on macOS

# What's Changed
This release improves navigation and usability with redesigned Previous/Next flows, one-click signing, and clearer transaction detail titles. It also fixes navigation and key‑caching issues, emailing and notifications, and includes several cleanup and configuration updates.

## Features
* feat: redesigned logic for Prev/Next flow by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2190
* feat: added Settings menu item and fix other items in Application menu  by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2199
* feat: improve UI of Previous/Next buttons by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2198
* feat: allow to sign and go to next transaction in a single click by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2214
* feat: more informative titles for transaction/group details pages by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2222
* feat: enhance automated update handling and improve conflict modal by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2225

## Fixes
* fix: Help item in Application menu should redirects to transactiontool.hedera.com by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2197
* chore: added interface definition to storeNetwork.ts by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2202
* chore: inlining createPersonalUser() and setupSafeNetwork() in storeUser.ts by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2203
* chore: unwrapping useStoreHelpers.deletePublicKeyMapping() by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2209
* chore: bump frontend version to 0.24.0 in configuration files by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2279
* fix: Cache new keys from account node updates for transaction signing

# What's Changed
## Features
* feat: handle mandatory update refusal by disconnecting from organization only not quitting app by @RAMTO in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2137
* 2112 transaction queries to use caches by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2161
* feat: transaction details page now displays external badge when transaction requires external signer by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2176
* feat: transaction/group table now displays external badge for transaction rows by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2181
* feat: TTv2 importing from TTv2 by @svienot in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2182
* feat: automated reconnect for disconnected backends by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2189

## Fixes
* 2110 add lastCheckedAt to cache tables and implement mirror node cache refresh cron job by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2139
* fix: fixed regression in TransactionsService.importSignature() by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2145
* History notifications not being removed by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2151
* chore: remove superfluous await and casting by @ericleponner in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2152
* fix: adjust websocket connection recovery settings and clean up code by @jbair06 in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2157
* docs: improve backend README with Kubernetes setup, troubleshooting, and fixes. by @deshmukhpranali in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/1782
* Fixed the select button text color issue in light/dark themes by @ddeskov-limechain in https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/pull/2174
* chore: misc refactoring

data.name: v0.18.1
data.body:
# What's Changed
## Fixes
* RabbitMQ to use quorum queues instead of classic queues for stability with intra-service communication
* All queue consumers now acknowledge receipt of message
* Removed transaction signer duplication bug
* Query transactions now rollback on failure during signature upload

## Versioning
* v0.18.1

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/compare/v0.18.0...v0.18.1

items.title: fix: Resolve transaction group signing performance bottlenecks
items.body:
Fixes #2409

## IMPORTANT

After the implementation of this PR, the bottlenecks are found to be in the Front End and the Infrastructure. This PR should be considered as providing suggestive changes and should not be merged just because it is ready. Consider coveting it to a draft!

---

## Summary

Resolves suspected performance bottlenecks when signing large transaction groups (50+ transactions), which previously caused 3–5 minute delays and Postgres connection pool exhaustion. After investigation, the root cause was identified as a possible combination of redundant work on both frontend and backend, missing database indexes, and absent connection safeguards — amplified by infrastructure constraints (slow mirror node, limited DB capacity).

This PR takes a targeted approach: reduce unnecessary load at every layer rather than adding concurrency that would multiply pressure on already-constrained infrastructure.

## What changed

### Database: Indexes + Connection Safeguards

- **Notification indexes** (`back-end/typeorm/migrations/1771500000000-NotificationIndexes.ts`):Adds 
  composite indexes on notification(type, entityId), notification_receiver(userId, isRead), and  notification_receiver(notificationId). These are the most-queried columns during signing flows — every transaction status change triggers notification lookups against these tables. Without indexes, these  were full table scans under load. Uses CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency.   

- **Connection timeouts** (`database.module.ts`): Adds `connectionTimeoutMillis: 10000` (fail fast if pool is exhausted instead of hanging indefinitely), `statement_timeout: 60000`, and `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout: 60000`. Previously there were no statement timeouts on the main pool, so a single slow query could hold a connection indefinitely, cascading into pool exhaustion.

- **Configurable pool sizes via env vars**: `POSTGRES_MAX_POOL_SIZE` and `POSTGRES_CACHE_POOL_SIZE` are now

items.title: feat: revise email notification system to reduce per transaction noise
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request introduces support for new transaction notification types (`FAILED` and `REJECTED`) and implements robust group email batching for transaction status updates. The batching logic ensures that users receive a single consolidated email per group when all relevant transactions reach the same lifecycle stage, reducing email noise and improving clarity. Additionally, the email sending infrastructure is made more resilient by ensuring all queued emails are flushed on service shutdown, and several configuration and test improvements are included.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2970 

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

items.title: fix: allow signing transactions in WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION status
items.body:
Fixes: [#2679](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2679)

**Description**:
Users were unable to sign transactions once they transitioned to the WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION status, even when they were otherwise permitted to add their signature.

**Related issue(s)**:
Two related issues surfaced this behavior:

#2357 — The "Sign" button was hidden on the Transaction Details page for any transaction in WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION, regardless of whether the current user was still a valid signer.
#2679 — The same blocking behavior appeared in the Transaction Group Details view: users could neither sign nor open the details of ready-to-execute transactions in a group.
This typically happens in multi-signer flows where a threshold key collects enough signatures to transition the transaction to WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION, but additional signers who are still eligible to sign (and should be allowed to) are locked out by the UI.

**Root Cause**
Two guard functions were overly restrictive, only treating `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` as a signable state:

`isSignableStatus` in `front-end/src/renderer/utils/transactionStatusGuards.ts`
Controls the `canSign` computed in `TransactionDetailsHeader.vue`, which gates the "Sign" button on the details page.
`isSignableTransaction` in `front-end/src/renderer/utils/transactionSignatureModels/index.ts`
Controls `canSign` on each row in `TransactionGroupRow.vue`, which gates signing inside the Transaction Group Details view.
Notably, the sibling guard `isApprovableStatus` **_already correctly includes_** both `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` and `WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION` — only the sign-side guards were inconsistent.

**Solution**
Updated both guards to treat `WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION` as a signable state, matching the behavior of `isApprovableStatus` and the intent described in the issues.

**Changes:**
`transactionStatusGuards.ts` — `isSignableStatus` now returns true for both `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` and `WAITING_FOR_EXECU

Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v43.0.0

Release Notes for v43.0.0

Stack Upgrades

  • Chromium 150.0.7871.46
    • New in 150
    • New in 149
  • Node v24.147.0
    • Node 24.17.0 blog post
  • V8 15.0

Breaking Changes

  • File downloads will open by default in the user's Downloads folder (or Home directory if Downloads doesn't exist). #49868
  • Fix: If a nativeImage was passed an image with a color profile, its pixel values will now be normalized to SRGB. This ensures that two visually identical images after color space application will receive similar pixel values when converted to a nativeImage. #51565
  • On Linux, frameless windows now have rounded corners by default, just like on macOS and Windows. Rounded corners can be disabled on all platforms by setting roundedCorners: false on the window. #52111
  • Removed showHiddenFiles support from the dialog API on Linux. #51880

items.title: fix: 2534 duplicate notifications from chain
items.body:
Fixes [#2518](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2518)

## Description

When multiple `chain` pods run in parallel, each pod runs the same cron
jobs on the same schedule. When a transaction expires or changes status,
all pods independently detect it and each publishes a notification event
to NATS. The notifications service processes all of them, resulting in
duplicate emails — one per pod.

## How it was reproduced

Locally with `docker compose up --scale chain=3`. The logs showed all 3
pods publishing the same status-update event:

chain-1 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 2
chain-2 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 3
chain-3 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 4
notifications-1 | Processing email batch... with 3 notifications in 1 groups

## Solution

Database-centric deduplication — no new dependencies (no Redis locks).

Every database UPDATE that precedes a notification emission now includes
a WHERE clause that checks the current status of the row:

```sql
UPDATE transaction
SET status = 'EXPIRED'
WHERE id = :id AND status IN ('NEW', 'WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES', ...)
RETURNING id;
```

The first pod to execute the UPDATE changes the row and gets it back via
RETURNING. The other pods find 0 matching rows (because the status
already changed) and skip the notification.

## Result after the fix

> chain-1 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 64
notifications-1 | Processing email batch... with 1 notifications in 1 groups
notifications-1 | Message sent: [7881ccba-...@gmail.com](mailto:7881ccba-...@gmail.com)

items.title: fix: validate signatures before recording in POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers
items.body:
**Description**:

With these changes, we make sure that the logic behind the `POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers` endpoint validates all submitted signature ((including already-signed keys) before they are recorded in the DB. 
Validation errors are logged and skipped, so a bad signature on one entry does not abort a batch upload (consistent with what is done for `importSignatures`)

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2929

**Notes for reviewer**:

- `validateSignature` utility function (libs/common/src/utils/sdk/transaction.ts)
  - Changed return type from `PublicKey[]` to `{ newPublicKeys: PublicKey[], allPublicKeys: PublicKey[] }`
  - Now validates all provided signatures against the transaction body, even if the key already signed
  - Throws "`Invalid signature`" if any signature bytes are invalid, before any writes occur
  -  Returns both all valid keys and only the newly-signed keys

- `SignersService.processTransactionSignatures` (apps/api/src/transactions/signers/signers.service.ts)
  - Calls `validateSignature` to catch invalid signatures
  - Only calls `addSignature` for keys in `newPublicKeys`

- `TransactionsService.importSignatures` (apps/api/src/transactions/transactions.service.ts)
  - Unwraps the new `validateSignature` return shape to use only `newPublicKeys` when updating transaction bytes

- Tests
  - Added unit tests for `validateSignature` covering new/already-signed/invalid-signature scenarios
  - Updated service-level tests to mock the new return shape
  - Added unit test to check that signers.service rejects and records nothing when validateSignature throws

items.title: fix: validate signatures before recording in POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers
items.body:
**Description**:

With these changes, we make sure that the logic behind the `POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers` endpoint validates the submitted signature bytes before they are recorded in the DB:
- `processTransactionSignatures()` now calls `validateSignature()` before calling `sdkTransaction.addSignature()`. The returned list of new and verified public keys replaces the previous nested map iteration which simplifies the loop. When the returned list is empty, we short-circuit the loop.
- validation errors are logged and skipped, so a bad signature on one entry does not abort a batch upload (consistent with what is done for `importSignatures()`)
 
 Tests added for both `processTransactionSignatures()` (throws on invalid signature) and `validateAndProcessSignatures()` (captures the error without re-throwing).
 
**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2929

items.title: fix: unauthorized errors after importing signatures from TTv1
items.body:
Fixes: [2552](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2522)

**Problem**
After importing User B's signature from a TTv1 zip, User B got 401 Unauthorized when opening the transaction details from Ready to sign or In progress. The History tab worked because executed transactions skip the access check.

Root cause: `importSignatures` only updated `transaction.transactionBytes` — it never inserted a `transaction_signer` row for the imported signer. `verifyAccess` grants access on five predicates (creator, observer, key-still-required, signer row, approver). Once B's signature was embedded in the bytes, B's key was no longer "required to sign", and with no signer row, all five predicates failed.

So it mutated the transaction's bytes (embedding User B's signature in the protobuf signature map) but never inserted a `transaction_signer` row for the imported signer. 

The same scenario does not break the in-app sign flow, because that flow goes through `SignersService.uploadSignatureMaps`, which does insert `transaction_signer` rows, dismiss pending sign-reminder notifications, and re-evaluate transaction status. _The TTv1 import path simply skipped all of that_.

**Solution**
Bring `importSignatures` to parity with `uploadSignatureMaps`:

- Insert `transaction_signer` rows for each imported signature — resolved from public key to `UserKey` (matched on both `toStringRaw` and `toStringDer`), deduped against existing rows.
- Atomic persistence — bytes update + signer insert + notification dismissal wrapped in a single `dataSource.transaction(...).` On rollback, all touched ids are marked `ErrorCodes.FST` and status recompute is skipped.
- Dismiss pending sign-reminder notifications for the owners of the imported keys.
- Skip no-op imports (same bytes + no new signer rows) to avoid spurious NATS events.
- Recompute transaction status post-commit so threshold-satisfied transactions advance to WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION. Emission split into chang

items.title: fix: organization transaction life cycle tests
items.body:
Summary

  - Re-enables the two previously-skipped tests in organizationTransactionLifecycleTests.test.ts ("Ready for Execution" and "History").
  - Replaces the API-based co-signing helper (signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi) with the original UI flow — log out, log in as each org user via logInAndSignTransactionByAllUsers, then log back in as the first user.

  Why

  After #2783 these two tests were temporarily skipped because the API-signing path was unstable for the AccountUpdate against a complex-key account: the transaction would stall in "Collecting Signatures" and never advance to "Ready
  for Execution"/"History", so the stage assertions timed out.

  Suspected front-end bug (why we stay on the UI path for now)

  We believe the API-signing failure is not in the automation helper but in the desktop app's handling of API-uploaded signatures for transactions whose key tree contains a nested threshold (the fixture key is 1 OF (user0_key, 2 OF 2
   (user1_key, user2_key))):

  - When users[1] and users[2] upload their signatures via POST /transactions/:id/signatures, the backend stores both TransactionSigner rows, but the FE's signature-progress evaluator appears to not recognize that the inner 2 OF 2
  threshold is satisfied — so the UI never marks the root threshold as satisfied and the executor never picks the transaction up.
  - The same multi-sig key works correctly when each user signs through the Ready to Sign UI flow: each click pushes the signature into the SDK transaction bytes via the same code path the executor reads, and the threshold evaluator
  agrees the key is satisfied. That's why the UI flow is reliable while the API flow is not.
  - Likely culprits to investigate in a follow-up:
    - the signature-aggregation/threshold-evaluation logic in the renderer (front-end/src/renderer/.../transactionSignatureProgress* or equivalent) that maps stored signer rows back onto the complex-key tree.
    - whether sdkTransaction.addSignat

## Summary

Expands UI automation coverage across the Hedera Transaction Tool by adding new Playwright scenarios, moving suitable UI checks into renderer component tests, refining the test infrastructure (per-describe parallel mode, JSON reporter, `.env` loading at config time, renderer test-hook escape hatch), and refreshing the release coverage matrix in `docs/test-scenarios.md`.

This branch changes **130 files** with **10,095 additions** and **2,903 deletions** across 36 commits.

## What changed

### New automated coverage

- **Transaction form validation** (`@local-basic`): transfer (5.5.7-5.5.11), header (5.14.6-5.14.8), file-ID (5.8.6/5.8.7/5.9.6/5.9.7/14.5.2), file-create owner-key (5.7.7), allowance owner/spender (5.6.5/5.6.6), complex-key duplicate (5.13.6).

- **Notification preferences** (`@organization-basic`): Ready to Execute, Signature Required, Cancelled toggles persist state changes (3.6.2-4 + 12.2.1-4).
- **Drafts and history table pagination** (`@local-transactions`): drafts table paginates at 10/page (4.3.3) and history table paginates at 10/page (4.4.4). Both tests seed records via the new `seedTransactionRecords` DB helper to avoid building 11+ rows through the UI.
- **Creator-cancels in-progress transaction** (`@organization-advanced`, 6.3.7): asserts a transaction creator can cancel a transaction while it is still in progress.

**Description**:
This pull request refactors and simplifies the version compatibility and upgrade flow in the frontend, with a focus on centralizing version checks, improving the handling of compatibility warnings, and enhancing the user interface for upgrade modals. The main changes remove redundant logic, delegate version checks to the appropriate components, and streamline how compatibility results are surfaced to users.

**Version check and compatibility logic refactor:**
- Removed version check and compatibility check logic from `GlobalAppProcesses.vue`, delegating these responsibilities to the `AutoLoginInOrganization` component and the version state store, reducing duplication and improving maintainability.
- Updated the test helper `setOrganizationVersionStateForTesting` to derive version status from the shape of the test data, ensuring the real computed pipeline is exercised and removing an unused test hook.

**Upgrade modal improvements:**
- Enhanced the `MandatoryUpgrade.vue` and `OptionalUpgrade.vue` modals to provide more informative and dynamic compatibility warnings, including improved messaging, summary, and warning slots, as well as better handling of multiple organizations requiring updates.
- In `MandatoryUpgrade.vue`, improved the proceed logic to disconnect all conflicting organizations before proceeding with the update, with error handling and user feedback.

**State management and UI consistency:**
- Both upgrade modals now use the centralized compatibility results from the version state store, improving consistency and reliability of compatibility information.
- The modals now respect the initial version check state to avoid showing upgrade prompts prematurely.

**Code cleanup:**
- Removed unused imports and logger instances, and eliminated dead code related to version checks and compatibility logic that is now handled elsewhere.

These changes collectively make the upgrade and compatibility experience more robust, maintaina

items.title: fix: automatically set notifications read after signing
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request introduces a new bulk update mechanism for notification receivers when transactions are signed, improves backend transaction processing, and simplifies related frontend notification handling. The backend now marks relevant notifications as read in bulk, and the frontend removes redundant notification logic, relying on backend updates.

**Backend enhancements:**

* Added `bulkUpdateNotificationReceivers` method to `SignersService`, enabling efficient bulk marking of notifications as read when transactions are updated.
* Integrated notification updates into the transaction signing flow: notifications are collected and updated alongside transaction bytes, ensuring consistency. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-45a2f3f118a08ffd6b7d3f4cecd2d83e838d56cde6cebdec2b57c3ff51d23d75R277) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-45a2f3f118a08ffd6b7d3f4cecd2d83e838d56cde6cebdec2b57c3ff51d23d75R296) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-45a2f3f118a08ffd6b7d3f4cecd2d83e838d56cde6cebdec2b57c3ff51d23d75R320-R324)
* Comprehensive tests for `bulkUpdateNotificationReceivers`, verifying query execution, parameter handling, and conditional behavior.
* Improved test naming for clarity in bulk update scenarios.

**Frontend simplification:**

* Removed notification store imports and notification marking logic from `TransactionNodeRow.vue`, as notifications are now handled by the backend. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bcd22789c7c11313f2fab68765b804f11f579ade8c0f653c405488084a059e7bL8) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-bcd22789c7c11313f2fab68765b804f11f579ade8c0f653c405488084a059e7bL44) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-bcd22789c7c11313f2fab68765b804f11f579ade8c0f653c405488084a059e7bL189-L209)
* Updated event handlers to directly emit transaction/group signed events, further streamlining the component.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2328

items.title: chore(transactions): batch UserKey lookups across DTOs on signature i…
items.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to PR #2745 (issue #2552). Replaces the N+1 UserKey query in `importSignatures` with a single bulk lookup. **_No behavioral change._**

## Problem

`importSignatures` issued one `entityManager.find(UserKey, ...)` per DTO. A 50-DTO batch hit the DB 50 times. Flagged by Copilot review on #2745, deferred to keep that fix scoped.

## Change

Restructured into two passes around one bulk query:

1. **Pass 1** — validate each DTO, mutate signatures, collect every public key (raw + DER) into a global Set.
2. **Bulk lookup** — one `find(UserKey, { where: { publicKey: In([...]) } })`, results bucketed into `Map<string, UserKey[]>`.
3. **Pass 2** — assemble signer rows per DTO from the in-memory map.

Buckets are arrays because `UserKey.publicKey` is indexed but not unique — two users can share a key, both rows must surface.

## Tests

- Single bulk lookup across multiple DTOs.
- Shared-publicKey rows both produce signer rows.
- Soft-deleted UserKey is excluded (`withDeleted` never set).

All existing tests still pass.

## Manual verification

User A creates a multi-sig tx, exports TTv1, then user B signs, User A imports the zip, User B logs in and sees full access.

items.title: feat: restore regression tests
items.body:
⏺ Description:                                                                                                                                                                 

Refactors automation page-objects to consolidate multi-step actions into composite methods, and replaces the slow UI-driven multi-user signing flow with a fast API-based path. Also fixes race conditions surfaced by the faster signing and re-enables the previously skipped organizationRegressionTests suite.
                                                                                                                                                                               
 - Page-object refactor: collapsed multi-call sequences into single intent-revealing methods on FilePage, SettingsPage, ContactListPage, OrganizationPage, and GroupPage;
 - API-based signing (automation/utils/api/): new Axios client (organizationApi.ts), ED25519 offline signer (offlineSign.ts), and orchestrator (signByAllUsersViaApi.ts). OrganizationPage.signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi and signTxByAllUsersAndRefresh now use this instead of logout → log in as each user → re-login as creator. items.title: fix: Investigate flaky backend cache query test in CI
items.body:
Fixes: [#2576](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2576)

**Description**:

`../../libs/common/src/sql/queries/cache.queries.spec.ts` is failing intermittently in CI, and it appears at least once on every PR. The failure output is minimal and does not provide enough context to diagnose the issue. We should start by enabling additional logging in the `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, `beforeAll`, and `afterAll` hooks to get more information about the test setup and teardown.

**Requirements**:

- Investigate the flaky failure in cache.queries.spec.ts.
- Add logging around beforeEach, afterEach, beforeAll, and afterAll to capture more context when the test fails.
- Improve test output so the root cause can be identified more easily in CI.
- Keep the test behavior unchanged aside from added diagnostics.

**Summary**:

The spec named in the issue (`cache.queries.spec.ts`) was not the cause. Two other specs in the same Notifications worker, `email.service.spec.ts` and `websocket.gateway.spec.ts`, were missing `jest.mock('ioredis')` (a pattern already adopted by `blacklist.service.spec.ts`, `scheduler.service.spec.ts`, and `redis-io.adapter.spec.ts`). As a result, every time their NestJS TestingModule instantiated EmailService / WebsocketGateway, the DebouncedNotificationBatcher constructor opened two real `ioredis` clients and issued `subscribe('__keyevent@0__:expired').` With no Redis on the CI runner, those clients burned through the default retry budget `(maxRetriesPerRequest: 20 × min(n*50, 2000)ms ≈ 10.5 seconds)` before throwing `MaxRetriesPerRequestError`. The error was attributed to whichever spec Jest considered "active" at that moment — usually `cache.queries.spec.ts`, because its Postgres testcontainer boot kept it on the clock for the full retry window.

**Root-cause fix**:

- `apps/notifications/src/email/email.service.spec.ts` — jest.mock('ioredis', ...) with a MockRedis class.
- `apps/notifications/src/websocket/websock

items.title: feat: use pnpm workspaces
items.body:
Fixes: [2751](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2751)

# Summary

The repo had three independent pnpm workspaces: `back-end/`, `front-end/`, `automation/`. Each shipped its own `package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

This caused four real problems:

- **Dependabot triplication** — every shared-dep bump opened 3 PRs.
- **Version drift** — shared deps could diverge silently between modules.
- **Slow CI** — three `pnpm install` calls per pipeline.
- **Cognitive overhead** — contributors had to know which directory to install in.

Goal: one root workspace, one lockfile, one catalog of shared versions.

# What changed

## Root workspace + catalog

- New `pnpm-workspace.yaml` at the repo root declares all packages.
- New root `package.json` holds a single dependency catalog.
- Child `package.json` files reference the catalog:
  ```json
  "axios": "catalog:"

- Deleted three child lockfiles and the old `back-end/pnpm-workspace.yaml.`
- Result: pnpm install at the root installs everything. `pnpm -F <name> <script>` runs scripts in any child.

## Dependabot consolidation
- `.github/dependabot.yml:` 4 ecosystems → 1 npm ecosystem at the root + github-actions.
- Added groups (hiero-ledger, nestjs, testing) so related bumps land in a single PR.
- This is the direct fix for the issue.

## CI workflows
Updated 4 workflows (test-backend, test-frontend, test-migrations, publish-docker-images):

- Install at the repo root, not per-module.
- Cache against the root `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
- Run scripts via `pnpm -F <module> <script>.`
- Docker build context flipped to `.` (repo root).

## Dockerfiles
Rewrote 4 Dockerfiles (api, chain, notifications, typeorm) on a consistent recipe:

- Build context is the repo root.
- Copy all workspace manifests + the root lockfile before install (keeps the install layer cacheable).
- Filtered install: `pnpm install --filter "back-end..." --filter "@back-end/api..."` — only what each ser

items.title: fix: multiple backend pods notifications
items.body:
Fixes [#2518](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2518)

**Description**:
The problem is that each pod runs the same cron jobs on the same schedule. When a transaction expires, all pods independently detect it and each publishes a notification event to NATS. The notifications service processes all of them, resulting in duplicate emails — one per pod.
NATS delivers messages exactly as it's told to. The problem is 3 pods all telling NATS to deliver the same message. By the time NATS is involved, it's too late — the duplicates are already created so the fix needs to happen before the NATS publish — at the cron job level.

I reproduced this locally by running `docker compose up --scale chain=3`. The logs showed:

> chain-1  | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 2
chain-2  | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 3
chain-3  | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 4
notifications-1  | Processing email batch... with 3 notifications in 1 groups

**The Solution**
I used Redis as a shared coordinator. Before each cron job runs its logic, it tries to set a key in Redis with the `NX` flag (only set if not exists). Only the first pod to reach Redis succeeds — the others see the key already exists and skip.

**Result After the Fix**

> chain-1  | [CronLock] Acquired lock for status_update_expired_transactions
chain-2  | [CronLock] Skipping status_update_expired_transactions — another pod holds the lock
chain-3  | [CronLock] Skipping status_update_expired_transactions — another pod holds the lock
chain-1  | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 50
notifications-1  | Processing email batch... with 1 notifications in 1 groups
notifications-1  | Message sent: <6d753e6e-...@limechain.tech>

items.title: fix: Do not broadcast cache refresh notifications when no data changed
items.body:
Fixes #2207 

**Problem**
The MirrorNode account endpoint uses very short-lived **etags** (~2 seconds). Because the cache refresh interval exceeds this window, every scheduled refresh receives an `HTTP 200` with a new **etag** rather than a `304 Not Modified `— even when the underlying account data is identical. The existing code treated every HTTP 200 as a data change, returning `REFRESHED` status and broadcasting a notification to all connected clients. This caused unnecessary client reloads and wasted network traffic on every refresh cycle, regardless of whether anything actually changed.

The node endpoint did not have this problem in practice (its **etags** are longer-lived and 304s work as expected), but it had the same architectural gap — no data-level comparison as a fallback when **etags** fail to suppress a refresh.

**Solution**
Added a data-level comparison layer in both `AccountCacheService` and `NodeCacheService` that runs after a successful `HTTP 200` fetch but before declaring the data as "refreshed":
- **hasAccountDataChanged(fetchedData, cached)** — compares the serialized key (via Buffer.compare) and receiverSignatureRequired field against the previously cached values.
 - **hasNodeDataChanged(fetchedData, cached)** — compares the serialized admin key and nodeAccountId against the previously cached values.

When the fetched data is identical to what was already cached, the service now returns `DATA_UNCHANGED` instead of `REFRESHED`. The `public refreshAccount()` / `refreshNode()` methods only return true for `REFRESHED`, so the upstream `CacheManagementService` correctly skips the notification when data hasn't changed.

A new `DATA_UNCHANGED` enum value was added to `RefreshStatus` to distinguish this case from `NOT_MODIFIED` (which represents an `HTTP 304 response`). The cache is still updated (timestamps, etag, claim release) in both cases — only the client notification is suppressed.

items.title: fix: Transaction components to handle live updates
items.body:
Fixes #2305 

**Problem**
Every `TRANSACTION_ACTION` WebSocket event triggers a full data refetch on every open transaction page for every connected user, regardless of relevance. This creates unnecessary server load and poor UX. Three root causes are:
1. **Global broadcast bug** (`fan-out.service.ts`): `FanOutService.notifyClients()` ignores the per-user `NotifyClientDto[]` array entirely and calls `this.websocket.notifyClient()` — a global Socket.IO `io.emit()` broadcast to ALL connected clients. The user IDs from the DTOs are discarded.
2. **Empty payload:** The event carries `content: ''` — zero information about what changed, which transaction, or what kind of change occurred.
3. **Blind refetch on frontend**: Every component subscribing to `TRANSACTION_ACTION` calls its full fetch function on every event with no filtering. The `useWebsocketSubscription` composable silently drops the payload (callback signature was `() => void`).

**Result:** If user A signs a transaction, ALL connected users on ALL transaction pages refetch ALL their data, with loading spinners flashing, even if the change is irrelevant to them.

---

**Solution:** Targeted Refetch with Metadata-Based Filtering
Enrich the WebSocket event with transaction/group IDs and event type, route it per-user only, and let each frontend page check relevance before refetching. This eliminates the "everyone refetches everything" problem while maintaining full backward compatibility.

Edge Cases Handled:
 - **Reconnection**: `useWebsocketSubscription` re-subscribes after reconnect via `wsStore.$onAction`. Missing events during disconnection are caught on the next relevant action.
 - **Batcher merging**: Multiple events within 500ms for same user merge content arrays. Parser unions all IDs across array items.
 - **Transaction not loaded**: If no matching IDs in current view → event is silently ignored (no refetch, no error).
 - **Pre-load state**: If a page hasn't finished its initial fetch

items.title: fix: caching  GET /transactions/{transactionId} (addendum)
items.body:
**Description**:

This PR is a follow-up of #2626.

Changes below add some logic for clearing transaction cache when notifications are received.
When a `TRANSACTION_NOTIFICATION` arrives, `EntityCache.forget()` is called to remove existing data from BackendTransactionCache. However this removal is conditional : data are removed only if they are 1s or more old.
This behavior enables to avoid clearing the cache when notification is received immediately after a full refresh.

**Related issue(s)**:

Contributions to #2521

**Notes for reviewer**:

```
M   front-end/src/renderer/caches/base/EntityCache.ts
    # Added 'strict' parameter to EntityCache.forget()
    # When strict == true, forget() behavior is unchanged : entry is removed from cache.
    # When strict == false, forget() removes data if it is older than 1s
    # Default value is strict = true.

M   front-end/src/renderer/pages/TransactionDetails/TransactionDetails.vue
M   front-end/src/renderer/pages/TransactionGroupDetails/TransactionGroupDetails.vue
M   front-end/src/renderer/pages/Transactions/components/History.vue
M   front-end/src/renderer/pages/Transactions/components/TransactionNodeTable.vue
    # In useWebsocketSubscription(), those view now invoke a specialized fetch():
    # fetchXXXOnNotif() calls regular fetchXXX() but after calling BackendTransactionCache.forget().
```

**Checklist**

- [ ] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

items.body:
Bumps [node-forge](https://github.com/digitalbazaar/forge) from 1.3.3 to 1.4.0.
Changelog

Sourced from node-forge's changelog.

1.4.0 - 2026-03-24

Security

  • HIGH: Denial of Service in BigInteger.modInverse()
    • A Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability exists due to an infinite loop in the BigInteger.modInverse() function (inherited from the bundled jsbn library). When modInverse() is called with a zero value as input, the internal Extended Euclidean Algorithm enters an unreachable exit condition, causing the process to hang indefinitely and consume 100% CPU.
    • Reported by Kr0emer.
    • CVE ID: CVE-2026-33891
    • GHSA ID: GHSA-5gfm-wpxj-wjgq
  • HIGH: Signature forgery in RSA-PKCS due to ASN.1 extra field.
    • RSASSA PKCS#1 v1.5 signature verification accepts forged signatures for low public exponent keys (e=3). Attackers can forge signatures by stuffing "garbage" bytes within the ASN.1 structure in order to construct a signature that passes verification, enabling Bleichenbacher style forgery. This issue is similar to CVE-2022-24771, but adds bytes in an addition field within the ASN.1 structure, rather than outside of it.
    • Additionally, forge does not validate that signatures include a minimum of 8 bytes of padding as defined by the specification, providing attackers additional space to construct Bleichenbacher forgeries.
    • Reported as part of a U.C. Berkeley security research project by:
      • Austin Chu, Sohee Kim, and Corban Villa.
    • CVE ID:

items.title: Notification System Fails When Transaction Requires Deleted User's Key
items.body:
**Summary**
* Fix notification failures when transactions require keys belonging to soft-deleted users
* Implement cascade soft-delete for user keys when a user is removed
* Add defensive filtering in notification and transaction services to handle edge cases

**Problem**
When a transaction required a key belonging to a soft-deleted user, the notification system would fail because:
1. NotificationReceiver entities were created for deleted users
2. TypeORM's soft-delete mechanism filtered out the user when sending emails
3. The notification process crashed due to missing user data
Solution: Hybrid Approach
This PR implements a combination of Option 1 (Filter by User Status) and Option 2 (Cascade Delete) from the [issue description](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2016).

**What's Implemented**
1. Cascade Soft-Delete (Option 2)
When a user is deleted, all their keys are now automatically soft-deleted:
```
// users.service.ts
async removeUser(id: number): Promise<boolean> {
  // Soft-delete all user keys first
  await this.repo.manager.softDelete(UserKey, { userId: id });
  // Then soft-delete the user
  await this.repo.softRemove(user);
}
```
2. Defensive Filtering (Option 1)
Added filtering at multiple points to handle historical data and edge cases:
* receiver.service.ts: Filters deleted keys/users before building notification recipient lists
* transactions.service.ts: Validates all required signers are active before creating transactions
The filter logic: !k.deletedAt && k.user && !k.user.deletedAt
3. Transaction Creation Validation
Blocks transaction creation if any required signer is inactive, with a clear error message listing affected users.

**Pros of Hybrid Approach**
* Defense in depth: Multiple layers of protection prevent failures at different stages
* No migration required: Filtering handles historical orphaned keys without database changes
* Clear user feedback: Transaction creation fails fa

items.title: feat: update size checks and smart collation for hip-1300 transactions
items.body:
Fixes [#2551](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2551)

Description:

Implements [HIP-1300](https://github.com/hiero-ledger/hiero-improvement-proposals/blob/main/HIP/hip-1300.md), which raises the transaction size limit from 6 KB to 128 KB when the fee payer is one of the privileged governance accounts: `0.0.2` (Treasury) or any account in `0.0.42` through `0.0.799`. All other transactions continue to use the standard 6 KB limit.

What changed:

- New helper (`privileged-payer.ts` on both backend and frontend) — single source of truth for `isPrivilegedFeePayer()` and `getMaxTransactionSize(feePayer)`.
- Backend size checks (`isTransactionOverMaxSize`, `isTransactionBodyOverMaxSize`, `smartCollate`) now derive the limit from the transaction's fee payer instead of using a **flat constant**. Smart collation no longer prunes signatures from a 50 KB transaction paid by `0.0.2`.
- Frontend split decision (`ValidateRequestHandler`, `BigFileOrganizationRequestHandler`, `BigFilePersonalRequestHandler`) is fee-payer aware, so an 8 KB File Update paid by treasury goes through as a single transaction instead of being split.
- Big-file chunk size is now also payer-aware: ~5,500 B for normal payers, ~130 KB for privileged payers — dramatically reducing the number of internal SDK chunks for large governance file updates and fixing a partial-upload failure mode for normal payers.
- Form input (`FileAppendFormData`) chunk-size `max` attribute is a `computed` driven by the currently selected fee payer.

How it works:

Every size check calls `getMaxTransactionSize(feePayer)`, which inspects the account's shard.realm.num and returns 131,072 bytes for `0.0.2` and `0.0.42–0.0.799`, or 6,144 bytes for everything else. The fee payer is extracted from the SDK transaction's `transactionId.accountId`, so no API signatures had to change — every existing call site automatically picks up the new behavior. Tests cover all boundary conditions (`0.0.1, 0.0.2

items.title: feat: parallel execution shared job
items.body:
**Description**:
This PR replaces the single shared E2E CI job with a remote shared-environment model so `@local-transactions`, `@organization-basic`, and `@organization-advanced` can run as separate rerunnable jobs while still provisioning `solo` and the back end only once per workflow run.
* Add remote shared E2E lifecycle scripts for provision, status, SSH tunnel setup, diagnostics collection, and cleanup
* Split front-end automation CI into `test-local-basic`, `provision-shared-e2e-env`, `shared-e2e-test`, and `cleanup-shared-e2e-env`
* Run `@local-transactions`, `@organization-basic`, and `@organization-advanced` as separate shared-env matrix jobs
* Keep suite jobs pointed at `localhost` by forwarding the remote organization API, Postgres, mirror node, and localnet node ports over SSH
* Allow suite job reruns to reprovision the remote shared environment automatically if it was already cleaned up
* Upload shared environment metadata and remote diagnostics as workflow artifacts
* Add a dedicated plan document for the external shared E2E environment architecture

**Description**:
This pull request updates the transaction export logic to clarify variable naming and ensure that transactions are only signed if they have no existing signatures. The changes affect both single and group transaction export flows, as well as the utility function responsible for generating export content.

**Transaction export improvements:**

* The `generateTransactionV1ExportContent` function now returns `transactionBytes` instead of `signedBytes`, and only signs the transaction if it does not already have signatures. This prevents unnecessary re-signing of already signed transactions.
* Variable names in both `TransactionDetailsHeader.vue` and `TransactionGroupDetails.vue` have been updated from `signedBytes` to `transactionBytes` to better reflect their contents and align with the updated utility function.

**Consistency and code clarity:**

items.title: feat: hip-1137 create registered node
items.body: Fixes: #2826 ## Description Adds HIP-1137 `RegisteredNodeCreate` support to the transaction tool. ## Why HIP-1137 introduces a new on-chain registry for non-consensus nodes (block nodes, mirror nodes, RPC relays, general services). The tool now lets users create those entries. ## Front-end - New menu entry **Registered Node Create** under the Node group, separated from existing items by a divider. - New page `Transaction/Create/RegisteredNodeCreate/` mirrors the Node Create flow (form + per-endpoint row editor). - Supports all 4 service-endpoint types per HIP-1137: Block Node, Mirror Node, RPC Relay, General Service. - Enforces ≥1 endpoint, ≤50 endpoints, description ≤100 UTF-8 bytes, IPv4 octet validity, port range [0, 65535]. - New `RegisteredNodeDetails.vue` for the history / details view. ## Back-end - New `RegisteredNodeCreateTransactionModel`. Tells the BE that the admin key is the required signer for this transaction type. - Registered in `TransactionFactory`. - `REGISTERED_NODE_CREATE` added to `TransactionType` enum (and the shared `BackEndTransactionType` so BE and FE stay in sync). - Added type-detection branches in `utils/sdk/transaction.ts` so the BE recognizes the new transaction class. - Refactored admin-key extraction in `transactions.service.ts` to dispatch through `TransactionFactory.getNewKeys()`. Adding future transaction types with admin keys is now just a model + factory entry — no service edit needed. ## Manual verification - Personal mode → testnet → returns `UNAUTHORIZED 157`. Expected: the payer is not a governance account, so testnet rejects the create. End-to-end pipeline confirmed working. ## Screenshots 1. Menu for the `Registered Node Create` 2. `Registered Node Create` page

items.title: fix: Ensure robust transaction cancelation handling
items.body:
## Problem

Transaction cancellation had several reliability and UX gaps:

- **Race conditions**: If a transaction's status changed between the initial read and the update (e.g., another user executed it), the cancellation could fail silently or throw raw errors.
- **No group-level cancel**: Canceling all transactions in a group required canceling each one individually, resulting in multiple API calls and no unified result reporting.
- **Partial failures left inconsistent state**: When canceling a group, a failure on one transaction could leave the UI showing stale data with no clear indication of what succeeded or failed.
- **Stale UI after errors**: If a single-transaction cancel failed, the frontend did not refresh from the server, so users could see outdated status.
- **Invalid actions on canceled transactions**: Users could still see Sign/Approve/Execute buttons on transactions that had already been canceled.
- **Raw error messages**: Backend exceptions could surface as technical error text instead of user-friendly messages.

## Solution

Harden cancellation across backend and frontend so cancel operations are idempotent, race-safe, and return clear user-facing outcomes, plus polish authorization, refresh behavior, and modal UX.

### Backend

- **Optimistic locking on single cancel** (`transactions.service.ts`): cancellation uses guarded status updates; if no row is affected, a fallback read distinguishes already-canceled (idempotent success) vs non-cancelable state (clear error).
- **Group cancel endpoint** (`PATCH /transaction-groups/:id/cancel`): classifies transactions in-memory, then performs a single bulk `UPDATE` with status guards. On partial-update (race condition), re-queries affected IDs to produce accurate per-item outcomes.
- **Creator-only group cancel enforcement** (`transaction-groups.service.ts`): backend now verifies the user is the creator of **all** transactions in the group before cancel-all is allowed.
- **Structured re

items.title: fix: certain email types not displaying subject
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request ensures all email notifications have a subject line. `TransactionExecuted` emails will now utilize the status code as is more accurate and less specific to application code. Additional values were added to the `additionalData` as needed for these changes, as well as future needs.

**Notification Type and Data Model Improvements**
* Added new notification email subjects for transaction expiration and user registration to the `NotificationTypeEmailSubjects` enum, expanding the types of notifications supported.
* Updated the transaction data model in `ReceiverService` to include new fields: `isManual`, `validStart`, and `statusCode`, enabling more granular notification logic and tracking.

**Transaction Executed Content Generation and Status Mapping**
* Refactored the `generateTransactionExecutedContent` test suite to map numeric `statusCode` values to transaction statuses, ensuring that codes 0, 22, and 104 are treated as `SUCCESS`, while other values default to `FAILED`. This improves the accuracy and clarity of transaction status representation in notifications.
* Updated tests for `newGenerateTransactionExecutedContent` to use `statusCode` instead of `status` in notification data, aligning with the new status code-based logic and ensuring consistent status handling across all test cases.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2403

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

items.title: Showing the freeze type on the transaction list table
items.body: **Problem** To help move faster during releases and in the narrow time window for abort transactions, the Transaction `Freeze` type needs to be added in the` Transaction Type` column of the transaction list table. **Solution** The backend does not store the specific freeze transaction type (Freeze Only, Prepare Upgrade, Freeze Upgrade, Freeze Abort) - it only stores the generic "FREEZE" type in the database. The freeze subtype information is only available within the serialized transactionBytes. To minimize the impact on the codebase and avoid backend changes, a **frontend-only approach** was implemented: 1. New utility function `getFreezeTypeForTransaction() `in **transactions.ts**: * When the frontend detects a freeze transaction, it makes an additional API call to `getTransactionById()` to fetch the full transaction details including transactionBytes * Deserializes the transaction bytes using the Hedera SDK * Extracts the `freezeType` code from the FreezeTransaction instance * Results are cached in a Map to avoid redundant API calls for the same transaction 2. New utility function `getDisplayTransactionType() `in **transactions.ts**: * Accepts either an SDK Transaction or Uint8Array * For freeze transactions, extracts and returns the specific freeze type string * Falls back to standard transaction type for non-freeze transactions 3. Updated components: * **TransactionNodeRow.vue** (Ready to Sign, In Progress, Ready for Execution, History tabs in organization mode): Added a watcher that detects freeze transactions and fetches the freeze type on-demand * **History.vue** (personal mode): Uses the existing body field from local transactions to extract freeze type * **Drafts.vue**: Parses the transactionBytes field to display specific freeze types for draft transactions **Before**

items.title: fix: Add logging framework & format to frontend
items.body:
## Add structured logging framework to the frontend

Fixes #2414 

### Problem

The frontend does not log anything. When users encounter problems there is no diagnostic trail they can share with developers, and developers cannot reproduce issues without guesswork. Every `console.log` scattered across the codebase is silently swallowed in production builds, so errors in websocket connections, transaction signing, database migrations, IPC calls, and version checks vanish without a trace.

### Why `electron-log` instead of `log4js`

The issue suggested evaluating `log4js` first. After investigation, `electron-log` was chosen because:

1. **Already available** — `electron-log` is a dependency of `electron-updater` (which the app already uses for auto-updates), so it introduces zero additional package overhead.
2. **Electron-native** — It understands Electron's process model out of the box (main, renderer, preload), handles `app.getPath('userData')` automatically, and integrates with Electron's crash reporter.
3. **Built-in rotation** — File rotation with configurable max size and archive count is included without additional plugins.
4. **Simpler IPC** — Renderer logs can be forwarded to the main process file transport through the existing preload bridge pattern, without needing a custom transport adapter that `log4js` would require in an Electron context.

### Solution

A structured JSON logging framework that writes to a single rotating log file with automatic sensitive-data sanitization.

**Architecture:**
- **Shared layer** (`shared/utils/logging.ts`) — log level types, argument normalization, key-based redaction, pattern scrubbing (JWT, Bearer, PEM), payload summarization, circular-reference detection, and depth-limited serialization.
- **Main process** (`main/modules/logger.ts`) — configures `electron-log` with a JSON file transport, log rotation (5 MB × 5 archives), console patching, and unhandled error capture. Exposes `createLogger(compone

items.title: feat: improve NATS connection resilience and error handling
items.body:
Fixes #2448


| Step | File | Before | After |
|------|------|--------|-------|
| **1. Connection state tracking** | `nats-jetstream.service.ts` | `connect()` blocks forever if NATS is down (`maxReconnectAttempts: -1` with no timeout). No way to check if NATS is connected. `getJetStream()` and `getManager()` always return non-null (assumes connection succeeded). | `connectWithTimeout()` races connection against a 30s timeout. If timeout wins, app continues booting in degraded mode. Added `connected` boolean, `isConnected()` method, `getConnection()` method. Return types are now `| null`. `monitorConnectionStatus()` updates `connected` flag on disconnect/reconnect events. |
| **2. Non-blocking startup** | `nats.module.ts` | Factory does `await service.onModuleInit()` — if NATS is unreachable, NestJS never finishes booting. No HTTP endpoints, no health checks, nothing works. | Factory still awaits `onModuleInit()`, but that now returns after 30s max (due to timeout in step 1). If NATS is down, logs a warning about degraded mode. The app boots and serves HTTP. |
| **3. Stream initializer resilience** | `nats-stream-initializer.service.ts` | Runs once on init with a `setTimeout(100)` hack. If NATS is down, throws error → can crash the service. No retry. | Removed the `setTimeout` hack. Retries up to 5 times with increasing delay (2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s). Checks `isConnected()` before attempting. If all retries fail, logs error but **doesn't crash** (streams likely exist from previous boot). |
| **4. Publisher connection awareness** | `nats.publisher.ts` | Always tries to publish. If NATS is down, catches error, logs at `error` level, returns `{ success: false }`. No fast-fail — waits for network timeout before failing. | Checks `isConnected()` before publish. If disconnected, returns `{ success: false }` immediately (no timeout wait). Log level changed from `error` to `warn` (NATS outages are expected operational events, not bugs). |
| **5. Emit helpers with await

items.title: fix: Improve cache refresh error handling for offline mirror nodes
items.body:
Fixes #2285

**Problem**
When a mirror node goes offline or becomes unavailable, the cache refresh mechanism blocks processing entirely. A single failing mirror node network stalls refresh operations for all networks, producing verbose and repetitive error logs that obscure actionable information. This means transaction processing can be delayed by infrastructure issues outside the system's control.

**Solution**
Implements a per-network circuit breaker pattern (`MirrorNodeCircuitBreaker` service) that isolates mirror node failures by network, preventing a single unhealthy network from blocking cache refresh for healthy ones.

Key Changes:
- Circuit breaker with three states (`CLOSED` / `OPEN` / `HALF_OPEN`): Tracks consecutive failures per mirror node network. After a configurable failure threshold (default: 3), the circuit opens and skips that network's refresh attempts entirely until a reset timeout elapses (default: 60s), at which point it transitions to half-open and probes with a limited number of attempts before fully recovering.
- Non-blocking, per-network processing: `refreshStaleAccounts() `and `refreshStaleNodes()` now group entities by `mirrorNetwork` and process each group independently. If one network's circuit is open, its entities are skipped while all other networks continue refreshing normally.
- Concurrency guard: A `refreshInProgress` flag prevents overlapping cache refresh executions, avoiding redundant work during slow refreshes.
- Aggregated error logging: Per-entity errors are collected into a `Set` per network and logged once as a single aggregate warning (e.g., `Account refresh failures for network "testnet": timeout; connection refused`), replacing repetitive per-entity error spam. 
- Configurable via environment variables:
   - `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD` (default: 3)
   - `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_RESET_TIMEOUT_MS` (default: 60000)
   - `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_HALF_OPEN_MAX_ATTEMPTS` (default: 1)

---

**Before:**
Mirror nod

items.title: fix: update transaction signing events mark notifications as read
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request updates the way sign events are emitted from the `SignSingleButton` and `SignGroupButton` components, so that they now include both the ID and the sign status (`signed: boolean`) in their payloads. Additionally, the parent `TransactionNodeRow` component is updated to handle these new payload structures, process notifications accordingly, and then emit only the ID upwards. This improves event handling clarity and allows for richer event data.

**Event Payload Structure Updates:**

- Changed the emitted payloads for the `transactionSigned` and `transactionGroupSigned` events in both `SignSingleButton.vue` and `SignGroupButton.vue` to include both the relevant ID and a `signed` boolean, instead of just the ID. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7e69ce8c6eccb28cac6573514167e79719498d722c3e86a1fa4badd56d2daff2L20-R20) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-657fdc9ec16b11ecd9ccfcd7be28eac905ed50bde88bad4200bfb88d07d44e46L21-R21)

- Updated the emit calls in the sign handlers to use the new payload structure. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7e69ce8c6eccb28cac6573514167e79719498d722c3e86a1fa4badd56d2daff2L63-R63) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-657fdc9ec16b11ecd9ccfcd7be28eac905ed50bde88bad4200bfb88d07d44e46L65-R65)

**Parent Component Event Handling:**

- Added `handleTransactionSigned` and `handleTransactionGroupSigned` methods to `TransactionNodeRow.vue` to process the new event payloads, mark notifications as read when appropriate, and then emit just the ID upwards.

- Updated the template in `TransactionNodeRow.vue` to use the new handler methods for the `transactionSigned` and `transactionGroupSigned` events.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2319

It also upgrades several dependencies in the frontend, including Electron and related build tools, to their latest versions. Additionally, the Dockerfiles now use a specific pnpm version for improved reproducibility, and minor documentation changes reflect these updates.

items.title: fix: transactions show in correct tabs
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request introduces several important updates to the cache management and transaction signature logic, as well as a database migration to support nullable fields. The changes improve clarity in logging, fix join conditions in SQL queries, extend signature key handling, and make the `nodeAccountId` field in the `CachedNode` entity nullable.

**Database and Entity Updates:**

* Made the `nodeAccountId` column in the `CachedNode` entity nullable, updating both the entity definition and adding a corresponding TypeORM migration to allow null values in the database. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7357f4fd39efd64d72d32b17f1b8771fdba6f263119d90c6285c1d9a0a52d215L21-R23) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1e19e9f2ede194537478c1e9fa88f639150372f5b0d26d43392a8329fba65ca3R1-R14)

**Cache Management Service Improvements:**

* Fixed SQL join conditions in `CacheManagementService` by replacing `"accountId"` with `"cachedAccountId"` and `"nodeId"` with `"cachedNodeId"` in relevant queries, ensuring correct data relationships. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6ef3203e473e7f511c85f121e5a48d3cbce4045d524c40567957113f0194199fL271-R271) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-6ef3203e473e7f511c85f121e5a48d3cbce4045d524c40567957113f0194199fL302-R302)
* Improved logging message in `CacheManagementService` to accurately state "accounts" instead of "nodes" when reporting refreshed entities.

**Transaction Signature Logic:**

* Updated the signature key generation in `TransactionSignatureService` to include any new keys from the transaction requirements, ensuring all necessary keys are accounted for.
* Refactored the extraction of signature requirements for clarity and directness in `TransactionSignatureService`.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2230
