items.title: Add notifications for user actions
items.body:
### Problem

**User Stories**

As a transaction signer, I need a way to view new tasks that are assigned to me.
As a transaction approver, I need a way to view new tasks that are assigned to me.

When a new transaction is created and shown to the user, a red notification icon should appear to indicate that there are new actions the user needs to take.

The red notification icon should display the total number of new actions the user has (e.g., the total number of transactions to sign , approve, etc).

If the user clicks on the tab showing the red notification icon, the icon should disappear.

If there are new tasks since the user last opened the application, the red notification icon should reflect the count of these new tasks since the last time the user clicked on it.

### Solution

See above

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Provide notifications for transaction signers and observers
items.body:
### **User Story**
As a transaction signer, I want to easily navigate to the organization and network where the transaction I need to review and sign is located when it's prepared for execution.


As a transaction observer, I want to know about the different stages a transaction goes through so I can stay up to date on its progress. 

### Acceptance Criteria

Note: Each time a user clicks on the notification type the application should take them to the right transaction status or history page in the right organization and network. 

**Notification for Successful Execution:**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification when the transaction is successfully executed on the network.
The notification message should state: Transaction executed successfully.
The notification should link to the history page when the user clicks on it.

**Notification for Transaction Failure:**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification if the transaction fails upon execution.
The notification message should state: Transaction failed.
Clicking on the notification should navigate the user to the transaction history with details about the failure.
The new entires in history should have a badge. 
The badge is removed when the user navigates away from the history page. 

**Notification for Transaction Expiration**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification if the transaction expires before receiving the required number of signatures.
The notification message should state: Transaction expired.
The notification should include a link to view the expired transaction details in the history tab when clicked.

**Notification for Transaction approval**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification if the transaction is approved by an approver. 
The notification message should state: Transaction approved [user].
The notification should include a link to view the expired transaction details in the history tab when clicked.

items.title: Add a remind button to send out transaction notification emails to those users who have not signed the transaction
items.body:
### Problem

Transactions can be scheduled for some time into the future. After a few days you may want to remind the users who have not yet signed the transaction. If we do that today, we have to compile a list of those users who did not sign, create an email, and send it off. It would be easier if there was a way in the app to click a button to remind the users who have not yet signed the transaction. For a future case, text message communication directly to users phones would be a neat option too.

### Solution

See above

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Notifications system
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request creates a scalable notification system, giving the flexibility to have many types of notifications.
The system consists of the following layers, which are currently in the Notifications microservice but could be (and better) split up into independent microservices for greater scalability:

- Receiver layer: It receives events from the message queue via its controller and computes the notification and receivers.
-  Fan-out layer: After computing the entities, they must be sent through the channels. Since we have preferences, the fan-out layer filters by checking the preferences for the passed notification.
- Processors layer: After the filtration by the fan-out service, the appropriate processors are called to send the notification to its receivers. Currently, they are the in-app processor and the old email. 

We are still not working with migrations, so please delete the current notification-preferences table and leave the Typeorm to synchronize the database.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #
#556

- [x] Documented (Code comments)
- [x] Tested (unit)

There were a few issues when migrating from docker (for local development) to kubernetes (for remote). Rabbitmq and Redis, for example, required extra work. These now have basic deployments that are functioning. The back-end server is running and can be tested against. 

Communication between services will now be done using both Rabbitmq, as well as REST calls. Rabbitmq will be used primarily for response-less communication (e.g. notifications), while REST will be used for requests requiring a response (authentication calls).

items.title: [Snyk] Upgrade @prisma/client from 5.10.2 to 5.18.0
items.body:
![snyk-top-banner](https://github.com/andygongea/OWASP-Benchmark/assets/818805/c518c423-16fe-447e-b67f-ad5a49b5d123)

Snyk has created this PR to upgrade @prisma/client from 5.10.2 to 5.18.0.

:information_source: Keep your dependencies up-to-date. This makes it easier to fix existing vulnerabilities and to more quickly identify and fix newly disclosed vulnerabilities when they affect your project.
- The recommended version is **671 versions** ahead of your current version. - The recommended version was released on **a month ago**.
Release notes
Package name: @prisma/client
  • 5.18.0 - 2024-08-06

    🌟 Help us spread the word about Prisma by starring the repo or tweeting about the release. 🌟

    Highlights

    Native support for UUIDv7

    Previous to this release, the Prisma Schema function uuid() did not accept any arguments and created a UUIDv4 ID. While sufficient in many cases, UUIDv4 has a few drawbacks, namely that it is not temporally sortable.

    UUIDv7 attempts to resolve this issue, making it easy to temporally sort your database rows by ID!

    To support this, we’ve updated the uuid() function in Prisma Schema to accept an optional, integer argument. Right now, the only valid values are 4 and 7, with 4 being the default.

Release notes
Package name: @prisma/client
  • 5.18.0 - 2024-08-06

    🌟 Help us spread the word about Prisma by starring the repo or tweeting about the release. 🌟

    Highlights

    Native support for UUIDv7

    Previous to this release, the Prisma Schema function uuid() did not accept any arguments and created a UUIDv4 ID. While sufficient in many cases, UUIDv4 has a few drawbacks, namely that it is not temporally sortable.

    UUIDv7 attempts to resolve this issue, making it easy to temporally sort your database rows by ID!

    To support this, we’ve updated the uuid() function in Prisma Schema to accept an optional, integer argument. Right now, the only valid values are 4 and 7, with 4 being the default.

items.title: Update the subject line for email notifications
items.body:
### Problem

The email subject line includes "Hedera Transaction Tool" for each notification type. This information is redundant as the sender column also includes a reference to the email bring from "Transaction Tool." The subject heading can be consolidated to include the action the user needs to take.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1e75094c-5084-48c9-8162-56b4d09e0e39)


You can see in these examples, the sender information is not repeated in the subject line of the email.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f49f9660-98ce-4ef2-a526-3e42b84bea68)



### Solution

Remove "Hedera Transaction Tool" from the subject line of the emails. 

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Send email notifications in the event the transaction has not received enough signatures and we are approaching the transaction valid start date
items.body:
### Problem

**User Stories:**

As a transaction creator, I need to know if we are approaching the transaction valid start time and do not have have enough signatures to remind signers to sign. 

The creator needs to know if we are approaching the transaction valid start date/time and do not have enough signatures to execute the transactions. A reminder should be sent to those signers who have not yet signed the transaction.

### Solution

See above

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Email notification should indicate which network is being used
items.body:
When a transaction notification is sent to users, there is no indication which network the transaction is on. 

Add network information to the email notification that is sent out.

items.title: Automatic App Update Notification
items.body:
Currently when there is a new release of the Hedera Transaction Tool Application the user has to manually navigate to "Check for updates" option. We can improve this flow by automatically notifying the user there is a new version of the App they are using and prompting them to upgrade it. 

We can add a "notification" like box on the right side of the logo with thin border which reads: 
"New version available" 
and has a button Update
We can use this (or similar) icon before the text - https://www.figma.com/design/GhUHiWVUBMRFfmL6gai5Mb/Transaction-Tool-Style-Guide?node-id=1245-7689&t=vAhRhgNZaWFq02xl-11

We can choose to have this one permanently there or to think of a way to close the notification, which will reappear once the user open the app again after closing it.

--------------

Another approach is to have a pop up appear informing the user there is a new version available on opening the app.
The user can choose to postpone or to proceed with the upgrade. 
This pop up should appear each time the app is opened unless the user specifies he/she doesn't wish to see the message again.

items.title: PoC Notification Controls
items.body:
With the Notifications (both email and in-app) we will need to provide users with the functionality to control (enable/disable) the notifications they wish to receive. For the PoC we can introduce one additional section in Settings page, which will be "Notifications". From there the user will be able to Enable or Disable the initial 2 types of Email notifications we are introducing:
- Transaction Threshold Reached / (as additional info text we can add) "You will be notified when a transaction you are a Signer in has collected enough signature to satisfy the threshold" - Enable/Disable toggle 
- Required Signature / Info text - "You will be notified whenever a transaction, which requires your signature has been created" - Enable/Disable toggle

items.title: Email Notifications - PoC
items.body:
For the purpose of introducing a more sophisticated Notification system across the Transaction Tool, we will initially start with two main email notifications, which users will receive when:
- Transaction Signature is required from the user -> This will be mostly useful for Transaction Signers 
- When all Signatures for a certain transaction has been received -> This info should be mostly useful for TX creators, but might also be welcomed by the TX signers as well

```[tasklist]

- [ ] https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/628
- [ ] https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/632
```

items.title: Send email notification when the transaction is executed successfully
items.body:
**User Stories:**

As a creator of the transaction, I need to know that the transaction executed successfully or if it failed and the reason why.
As a transaction signer, I need to know the transaction executed successfully. 

The content should include
- A link to the successful transaction in HashScan
- If failed, communicating the nature of the failure

items.title: Consolidate the number of .env files that store the same values
items.body:
### Problem


There are a few locations where the following values are repeated:
```
# Postgres database
# service name for postgres service in docker
POSTGRES_HOST=database
POSTGRES_PORT=5432
POSTGRES_DATABASE=postgres
POSTGRES_USERNAME=postgres
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres
# should be false in production
POSTGRES_SYNCHRONIZE=true

# PG Admin settings
PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL=admin@admin.com
PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD=pgadmin4

NODE_ENV=development
```
```

API: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/blob/main/back-end/apps/api/.env.test
Chain: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/blob/main/back-end/apps/chain/example.env
Notifications: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/blob/main/back-end/apps/notifications/example.env
backend Root: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/blob/main/back-end/example.env


### Solution

It seems it would make sense to house these values in one location to call from vs. in multiple locations. 

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Update backend to manage version updates for the front end
items.body:
### Problem

As an admin of the transaction tool, I want to be in control of what versions get deployed to my users.

When I decide to upgrade the backend to specific version number, I want the corresponding front end version that is compatible with the backend to be prompted to the user to update to.



### Solution

The backend version should have a list for the compatible front end versions so that when an admin updates the backend the users can be notified accordingly for which front end version to download and install. 

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Contact list updates
items.body:
### Problem

- Show everyone in any of your organizations or that you have added personally in the contact list
- Organizations will have contacts in their backend, and will be shown as notifications to users to add to their contact list
- The manual/personal contacts added are stored locally and not in the organization backend
- A contact entry can have more then one public key associated with a contact


**Notes**
- DB adds new table for public key and user name in contact list
- Add organization column to the contact list table UI
- Nicknames are not stored on the server so we need to store that information locally whether they are manually added or added by the organization
- Organization and username email is unique per organization
- Two different organizations can have the same email
- Key Nick Name does not have to be unique when stored locally
- Observer and Approver groups are stored locally and created by the user

### Solution

See above

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: chore(deps): bump amqplib from 0.10.9 to 2.0.1 in the messaging group across 1 directory
items.body:
Bumps the messaging group with 1 update in the / directory: [amqplib](https://github.com/amqp-node/amqplib). Updates `amqplib` from 0.10.9 to 2.0.1
Release notes

Sourced from amqplib's releases.

v2.0.1

  • Remove buffer-more-ints dependency; use Node.js built-in BigInt Buffer methods (readBigInt64BE, readBigUInt64BE, writeBigInt64BE, writeBigUInt64BE) which have been available since Node.js v10.4

v2.0.0

BREAKING CHANGE

heartbeat: 0 now correctly disables heartbeats, sending 0 in the ConnectionTuneOk frame regardless of the server's suggestion. This aligns with the AMQP 0-9-1 spec, which defines heartbeat=0 as "disabled".

Previously, 0 was passed through negotiate() which treated it as "no preference", causing the server's suggested value to be used instead.

Migration: if you are passing heartbeat: 0 and want to preserve the old behaviour of accepting the server's value, omit the option or pass null instead.

Fixes #467. Supersedes #469.

1.2.0

Add bundled TypeScript type definitions (fixes #843)

For full history see CHANGELOG.md.

Changelog

Sourced from amqplib's changelog.

v2.0.1

  • Remove

items.title: feat: extend automation coverage
items.body:
# Expand E2E and Renderer Test Coverage

## Summary

Expands UI automation coverage across the Hedera Transaction Tool by adding new Playwright scenarios, moving suitable UI checks into renderer component tests, refining the test infrastructure (per-describe parallel mode, JSON reporter, `.env` loading at config time, renderer test-hook escape hatch), and refreshing the release coverage matrix in `docs/test-scenarios.md`.

This branch changes **130 files** with **10,095 additions** and **2,903 deletions** across 36 commits.

## What changed

### New automated coverage

- **Transaction form validation** (`@local-basic`): transfer (5.5.7-5.5.11), header (5.14.6-5.14.8), file-ID (5.8.6/5.8.7/5.9.6/5.9.7/14.5.2), file-create owner-key (5.7.7), allowance owner/spender (5.6.5/5.6.6), complex-key duplicate (5.13.6).
- **Account ID checksum** on Link Existing Account (14.5.1) appended to `workflowTests.test.ts` (`@local-transactions` — needs mirror node).
- **Settings → Organizations tab** (`@local-basic`): table headers (3.4.4), connection status badge for connected / disconnected / upgradeRequired (3.4.5), version info badges (3.4.6), disconnect-reason note (13.2.3), blocked org selection (13.2.4), global Update Available modal (15.1.1).
- **Notification preferences** (`@organization-basic`): Ready to Execute, Signature Required, Cancelled toggles persist state changes (3.6.2-4 + 12.2.1-4).
- **Drafts and history table pagination** (`@local-transactions`): drafts table paginates at 10/page (4.3.3) and history table paginates at 10/page (4.4.4). Both tests seed records via the new `seedTransactionRecords` DB helper to avoid building 11+ rows through the UI.
- **Creator-cancels in-progress transaction** (`@organization-advanced`, 6.3.7): asserts a transaction creator can cancel a transaction while it is still in progress.
- Earlier in the branch: account reset, login/session, settings, keys, files, transaction drafts/files, account create/update/delete/dat

Several Vue components received small adjustments (test hooks, prop wiring, conditional rendering fixes) to make them deterministic under test.
                                                                                                                                                                               
  - Add page-level specs for Accounts, ContactList, Files, Transactions, Drafts, OrganizationLogin, and Settings (General, Profile, PublicKeys, GeneralControls)               
  - Add component specs for ContactDetails, TransactionSelectionModal, LinkExistingAccount, LinkExistingFile, CreateTransactionGroup, and EmailLoginForm
  - Extend existing specs for TransactionDetailsHeader and storeTransactionGroup                                                                                               
  - Adjust renderer components (Accounts, Files, ContactList, SignUpUser, Drafts, History, Settings tabs, modals) to support testability without altering behavior             
  - Wire up minor renderer/main.ts setup needed by the new specs                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                               
  Related issue(s):

items.title: Fix transaction lifecycle notifications overriding "new" status
items.body:
**Description:**  
Transaction lifecycle notifications can override each other, causing users to miss new transactions. If a transaction requires 2-of-3 signatures and users B and C sign before user A sees it, user A loses the "new transaction" notification. The transaction moves silently from "new" to "ready to sign" without user A ever being notified.  

**Current behavior:**  
```
Tx requires 2/3 signatures (A,B,C)
→ B and C sign before A sees it
→ A sees: "Ready to sign" tab (no "new" notification)
→ A misses notification entirely
```

**Expected behavior:**  
```
Tx requires 2/3 signatures (A,B,C)
→ B and C sign before A sees it  
→ A still sees: "New transaction" notification
→ Notification persists until A acts on it (signs, dismisses, etc.)
```

**Root cause:**  
- Notification state transitions automatically (`new` → `ready to sign`)  
- Once overridden, "new" notification is lost even for eligible users  
- Users only see final state, not that they missed critical lifecycle events  

**Scope:**  
- Persist "new transaction" notification for eligible users until they **personally act** on it  
- Decouple notification state from transaction lifecycle state  
- Track per-user notification state independently  

**Acceptance criteria:**  
- [ ] Users see "new transaction" notification until they personally interact with it  
- [ ] Notification persists even if other users advance transaction state  
- [ ] 2-of-3 scenario: all 3 users see "new" notification until each acts  
- [ ] Notification clears only after user action (sign, dismiss) or final state (executed, expired, canceled)  
- [ ] "Ready to sign" notifications work independently of "new" notifications

items.title: Verify transaction group valid_start override behavior
items.body:
**Description**:  
Observed transaction group validStart value overriding individual transaction validStart values. Unable to reproduce initially, but observed again days later. Confirm this is not a regression vs user error (wrong branch/environment) and ensure group settings never override individual transaction validStart values unless the transaction validStart is < group validStart.

**Investigation steps:**
- Review recent code changes affecting validStart handling
- Document exact reproduction steps when observed

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Group validStart never overrides individual transaction validStart unless group validStart > transaction validStart
- Clear reproduction steps documented (or confirmed as user error)

items.title: fix: validate signatures before recording in POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers
items.body:
**Description**:

With these changes, we make sure that the logic behind the `POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers` endpoint validates all submitted signature ((including already-signed keys) before they are recorded in the DB. 
Validation errors are logged and skipped, so a bad signature on one entry does not abort a batch upload (consistent with what is done for `importSignatures`)

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2929

**Notes for reviewer**:

- `validateSignature` utility function (libs/common/src/utils/sdk/transaction.ts)
  - Changed return type from `PublicKey[]` to `{ newPublicKeys: PublicKey[], allPublicKeys: PublicKey[] }`
  - Now validates all provided signatures against the transaction body, even if the key already signed
  - Throws "`Invalid signature`" if any signature bytes are invalid, before any writes occur
  -  Returns both all valid keys and only the newly-signed keys

- `SignersService.processTransactionSignatures` (apps/api/src/transactions/signers/signers.service.ts)
  - Calls `validateSignature` to catch invalid signatures
  - Only calls `addSignature` for keys in `newPublicKeys`

- `TransactionsService.importSignatures` (apps/api/src/transactions/transactions.service.ts)
  - Unwraps the new `validateSignature` return shape to use only `newPublicKeys` when updating transaction bytes

- Tests
  - Added unit tests for `validateSignature` covering new/already-signed/invalid-signature scenarios
  - Updated service-level tests to mock the new return shape
  - Added unit test to check that signers.service rejects and records nothing when validateSignature throws

items.body: **Description**: Finalize support for non organizational signature support (most of it was already implemented): - activate feature flag to display "External" badge on transactions/required signatures -- this only for the creator the tx - replace the `Export` transaction action (which currently allows to choose the format between 'tx' and 'tx2') by 2 distinct actions: - `Export` for 'tx2' format - `Export (Transaction Tool 1.0)` for 'tx' format - rename the `Export All` group action to `Export All (Transaction Tool 1.0)` Unrelated: add new pnpm scripts. **Related issue(s)**: Fixes #2786 **UI workflow**: 1. Transaction creator (org member) creates a node create/update transaction requiring a signature from a non-org signer. Such transaction, and the corresponding non-org key are signaled by an `External` badge: 2. Transaction creator exports the transaction and sends it off-chain to the non-org signer. 3. Non-org signer, using the transaction tool in personal mode, selects `Sign Transactions from File` action. The transaction to sign is displayed only if the non-org user possesses a key required to sign. 4. Non-org signer selects `Sign and Update File` action to update the transaction file in place and re

items.title: fix: validate signatures before recording in POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers
items.body:
**Description**:

With these changes, we make sure that the logic behind the `POST /transactions/:transactionId/signers` endpoint validates the submitted signature bytes before they are recorded in the DB:
- `processTransactionSignatures()` now calls `validateSignature()` before calling `sdkTransaction.addSignature()`. The returned list of new and verified public keys replaces the previous nested map iteration which simplifies the loop. When the returned list is empty, we short-circuit the loop.
- validation errors are logged and skipped, so a bad signature on one entry does not abort a batch upload (consistent with what is done for `importSignatures()`)
 
 Tests added for both `processTransactionSignatures()` (throws on invalid signature) and `validateAndProcessSignatures()` (captures the error without re-throwing).
 
**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2929

items.title: fix: Resolve transaction group signing performance bottlenecks
items.body:
Fixes #2409

## IMPORTANT

After the implementation of this PR, the bottlenecks are found to be in the Front End and the Infrastructure. This PR should be considered as providing suggestive changes and should not be merged just because it is ready. Consider coveting it to a draft!

---

## Summary

Resolves suspected performance bottlenecks when signing large transaction groups (50+ transactions), which previously caused 3–5 minute delays and Postgres connection pool exhaustion. After investigation, the root cause was identified as a possible combination of redundant work on both frontend and backend, missing database indexes, and absent connection safeguards — amplified by infrastructure constraints (slow mirror node, limited DB capacity).

This PR takes a targeted approach: reduce unnecessary load at every layer rather than adding concurrency that would multiply pressure on already-constrained infrastructure.

## What changed

### Database: Indexes + Connection Safeguards

- **Notification indexes** (`back-end/typeorm/migrations/1771500000000-NotificationIndexes.ts`):Adds 
  composite indexes on notification(type, entityId), notification_receiver(userId, isRead), and  notification_receiver(notificationId). These are the most-queried columns during signing flows — every transaction status change triggers notification lookups against these tables. Without indexes, these  were full table scans under load. Uses CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency.   

- **Connection timeouts** (`database.module.ts`): Adds `connectionTimeoutMillis: 10000` (fail fast if pool is exhausted instead of hanging indefinitely), `statement_timeout: 60000`, and `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout: 60000`. Previously there were no statement timeouts on the main pool, so a single slow query could hold a connection indefinitely, cascading into pool exhaustion.

- **Configurable pool sizes via env vars**: `POSTGRES_MAX_POOL_SIZE` and `POSTGRES_CACHE_POOL_SIZE` are now

items.title: fix: unauthorized errors after importing signatures from TTv1
items.body:
Fixes: [2552](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2522)

**Problem**
After importing User B's signature from a TTv1 zip, User B got 401 Unauthorized when opening the transaction details from Ready to sign or In progress. The History tab worked because executed transactions skip the access check.

Root cause: `importSignatures` only updated `transaction.transactionBytes` — it never inserted a `transaction_signer` row for the imported signer. `verifyAccess` grants access on five predicates (creator, observer, key-still-required, signer row, approver). Once B's signature was embedded in the bytes, B's key was no longer "required to sign", and with no signer row, all five predicates failed.

So it mutated the transaction's bytes (embedding User B's signature in the protobuf signature map) but never inserted a `transaction_signer` row for the imported signer. 

The same scenario does not break the in-app sign flow, because that flow goes through `SignersService.uploadSignatureMaps`, which does insert `transaction_signer` rows, dismiss pending sign-reminder notifications, and re-evaluate transaction status. _The TTv1 import path simply skipped all of that_.

**Solution**
Bring `importSignatures` to parity with `uploadSignatureMaps`:

- Insert `transaction_signer` rows for each imported signature — resolved from public key to `UserKey` (matched on both `toStringRaw` and `toStringDer`), deduped against existing rows.
- Atomic persistence — bytes update + signer insert + notification dismissal wrapped in a single `dataSource.transaction(...).` On rollback, all touched ids are marked `ErrorCodes.FST` and status recompute is skipped.
- Dismiss pending sign-reminder notifications for the owners of the imported keys.
- Skip no-op imports (same bytes + no new signer rows) to avoid spurious NATS events.
- Recompute transaction status post-commit so threshold-satisfied transactions advance to WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION. Emission split into chang

items.title: chore(transactions): batch UserKey lookups across DTOs on signature i…
items.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to PR #2745 (issue #2552). Replaces the N+1 UserKey query in `importSignatures` with a single bulk lookup. **_No behavioral change._**

## Problem

`importSignatures` issued one `entityManager.find(UserKey, ...)` per DTO. A 50-DTO batch hit the DB 50 times. Flagged by Copilot review on #2745, deferred to keep that fix scoped.

## Change

Restructured into two passes around one bulk query:

1. **Pass 1** — validate each DTO, mutate signatures, collect every public key (raw + DER) into a global Set.
2. **Bulk lookup** — one `find(UserKey, { where: { publicKey: In([...]) } })`, results bucketed into `Map<string, UserKey[]>`.
3. **Pass 2** — assemble signer rows per DTO from the in-memory map.

Buckets are arrays because `UserKey.publicKey` is indexed but not unique — two users can share a key, both rows must surface.

## Tests

- Single bulk lookup across multiple DTOs.
- Shared-publicKey rows both produce signer rows.
- Soft-deleted UserKey is excluded (`withDeleted` never set).

All existing tests still pass.

## Manual verification

User A creates a multi-sig tx, exports TTv1, then user B signs, User A imports the zip, User B logs in and sees full access.

items.title: fix: organization transaction life cycle tests
items.body:
Summary

  - Re-enables the two previously-skipped tests in organizationTransactionLifecycleTests.test.ts ("Ready for Execution" and "History").
  - Replaces the API-based co-signing helper (signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi) with the original UI flow — log out, log in as each org user via logInAndSignTransactionByAllUsers, then log back in as the first user.

  Why

  After #2783 these two tests were temporarily skipped because the API-signing path was unstable for the AccountUpdate against a complex-key account: the transaction would stall in "Collecting Signatures" and never advance to "Ready
  for Execution"/"History", so the stage assertions timed out.

  Suspected front-end bug (why we stay on the UI path for now)

  We believe the API-signing failure is not in the automation helper but in the desktop app's handling of API-uploaded signatures for transactions whose key tree contains a nested threshold (the fixture key is 1 OF (user0_key, 2 OF 2
   (user1_key, user2_key))):

  - When users[1] and users[2] upload their signatures via POST /transactions/:id/signatures, the backend stores both TransactionSigner rows, but the FE's signature-progress evaluator appears to not recognize that the inner 2 OF 2
  threshold is satisfied — so the UI never marks the root threshold as satisfied and the executor never picks the transaction up.
  - The same multi-sig key works correctly when each user signs through the Ready to Sign UI flow: each click pushes the signature into the SDK transaction bytes via the same code path the executor reads, and the threshold evaluator
  agrees the key is satisfied. That's why the UI flow is reliable while the API flow is not.
  - Likely culprits to investigate in a follow-up:
    - the signature-aggregation/threshold-evaluation logic in the renderer (front-end/src/renderer/.../transactionSignatureProgress* or equivalent) that maps stored signer rows back onto the complex-key tree.
    - whether sdkTransaction.addSignat

items.title: fix: allow signing transactions in WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION status
items.body:
Fixes: [#2679](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2679)

**Description**:
Users were unable to sign transactions once they transitioned to the WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION status, even when they were otherwise permitted to add their signature.

**Related issue(s)**:
Two related issues surfaced this behavior:

#2357 — The "Sign" button was hidden on the Transaction Details page for any transaction in WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION, regardless of whether the current user was still a valid signer.
#2679 — The same blocking behavior appeared in the Transaction Group Details view: users could neither sign nor open the details of ready-to-execute transactions in a group.
This typically happens in multi-signer flows where a threshold key collects enough signatures to transition the transaction to WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION, but additional signers who are still eligible to sign (and should be allowed to) are locked out by the UI.

**Root Cause**
Two guard functions were overly restrictive, only treating `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` as a signable state:

`isSignableStatus` in `front-end/src/renderer/utils/transactionStatusGuards.ts`
Controls the `canSign` computed in `TransactionDetailsHeader.vue`, which gates the "Sign" button on the details page.
`isSignableTransaction` in `front-end/src/renderer/utils/transactionSignatureModels/index.ts`
Controls `canSign` on each row in `TransactionGroupRow.vue`, which gates signing inside the Transaction Group Details view.
Notably, the sibling guard `isApprovableStatus` **_already correctly includes_** both `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` and `WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION` — only the sign-side guards were inconsistent.

**Solution**
Updated both guards to treat `WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION` as a signable state, matching the behavior of `isApprovableStatus` and the intent described in the issues.

**Changes:**
`transactionStatusGuards.ts` — `isSignableStatus` now returns true for both `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` and `WAITING_FOR_EXECU

items.title: chore(deps): bump node-forge from 1.3.3 to 1.4.0 in /front-end
items.body:
Bumps [node-forge](https://github.com/digitalbazaar/forge) from 1.3.3 to 1.4.0.
Changelog

Sourced from node-forge's changelog.

1.4.0 - 2026-03-24

Security

  • HIGH: Denial of Service in BigInteger.modInverse()
    • A Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability exists due to an infinite loop in the BigInteger.modInverse() function (inherited from the bundled jsbn library). When modInverse() is called with a zero value as input, the internal Extended Euclidean Algorithm enters an unreachable exit condition, causing the process to hang indefinitely and consume 100% CPU.
    • Reported by Kr0emer.
    • CVE ID: CVE-2026-33891
    • GHSA ID: GHSA-5gfm-wpxj-wjgq
  • HIGH: Signature forgery in RSA-PKCS due to ASN.1 extra field.
    • RSASSA PKCS#1 v1.5 signature verification accepts forged signatures for low public exponent keys (e=3). Attackers can forge signatures by stuffing "garbage" bytes within the ASN.1 structure in order to construct a signature that passes verification, enabling Bleichenbacher style forgery. This issue is similar to CVE-2022-24771, but adds bytes in an addition field within the ASN.1 structure, rather than outside of it.
    • Additionally, forge does not validate that signatures include a minimum of 8 bytes of padding as defined by the specification, providing attackers additional space to construct Bleichenbacher forgeries.
    • Reported as part of a U.C. Berkeley security research project by:
      • Austin Chu, Sohee Kim, and Corban Villa.
    • CVE ID:

items.title: feat: update size checks and smart collation for hip-1300 transactions
items.body:
Fixes [#2551](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2551)

Description:

Implements [HIP-1300](https://github.com/hiero-ledger/hiero-improvement-proposals/blob/main/HIP/hip-1300.md), which raises the transaction size limit from 6 KB to 128 KB when the fee payer is one of the privileged governance accounts: `0.0.2` (Treasury) or any account in `0.0.42` through `0.0.799`. All other transactions continue to use the standard 6 KB limit.

What changed:

- New helper (`privileged-payer.ts` on both backend and frontend) — single source of truth for `isPrivilegedFeePayer()` and `getMaxTransactionSize(feePayer)`.
- Backend size checks (`isTransactionOverMaxSize`, `isTransactionBodyOverMaxSize`, `smartCollate`) now derive the limit from the transaction's fee payer instead of using a **flat constant**. Smart collation no longer prunes signatures from a 50 KB transaction paid by `0.0.2`.
- Frontend split decision (`ValidateRequestHandler`, `BigFileOrganizationRequestHandler`, `BigFilePersonalRequestHandler`) is fee-payer aware, so an 8 KB File Update paid by treasury goes through as a single transaction instead of being split.
- Big-file chunk size is now also payer-aware: ~5,500 B for normal payers, ~130 KB for privileged payers — dramatically reducing the number of internal SDK chunks for large governance file updates and fixing a partial-upload failure mode for normal payers.
- Form input (`FileAppendFormData`) chunk-size `max` attribute is a `computed` driven by the currently selected fee payer.

How it works:

Every size check calls `getMaxTransactionSize(feePayer)`, which inspects the account's shard.realm.num and returns 131,072 bytes for `0.0.2` and `0.0.42–0.0.799`, or 6,144 bytes for everything else. The fee payer is extracted from the SDK transaction's `transactionId.accountId`, so no API signatures had to change — every existing call site automatically picks up the new behavior. Tests cover all boundary conditions (`0.0.1, 0.0.2

items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request updates the transaction export logic to clarify variable naming and ensure that transactions are only signed if they have no existing signatures. The changes affect both single and group transaction export flows, as well as the utility function responsible for generating export content.

**Transaction export improvements:**

* The `generateTransactionV1ExportContent` function now returns `transactionBytes` instead of `signedBytes`, and only signs the transaction if it does not already have signatures. This prevents unnecessary re-signing of already signed transactions.
* Variable names in both `TransactionDetailsHeader.vue` and `TransactionGroupDetails.vue` have been updated from `signedBytes` to `transactionBytes` to better reflect their contents and align with the updated utility function.

**Consistency and code clarity:**

* The JSON content creation in `generateTransactionV1ExportContent` was slightly refactored for clarity, with direct use of properties and inline formatting.

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2535

items.title: feat: hip-1137 create registered node
items.body: Fixes: #2826 ## Description Adds HIP-1137 `RegisteredNodeCreate` support to the transaction tool. ## Why HIP-1137 introduces a new on-chain registry for non-consensus nodes (block nodes, mirror nodes, RPC relays, general services). The tool now lets users create those entries. ## Front-end - New menu entry **Registered Node Create** under the Node group, separated from existing items by a divider. - New page `Transaction/Create/RegisteredNodeCreate/` mirrors the Node Create flow (form + per-endpoint row editor). - Supports all 4 service-endpoint types per HIP-1137: Block Node, Mirror Node, RPC Relay, General Service. - Enforces ≥1 endpoint, ≤50 endpoints, description ≤100 UTF-8 bytes, IPv4 octet validity, port range [0, 65535]. - New `RegisteredNodeDetails.vue` for the history / details view. ## Back-end - New `RegisteredNodeCreateTransactionModel`. Tells the BE that the admin key is the required signer for this transaction type. - Registered in `TransactionFactory`. - `REGISTERED_NODE_CREATE` added to `TransactionType` enum (and the shared `BackEndTransactionType` so BE and FE stay in sync). - Added type-detection branches in `utils/sdk/transaction.ts` so the BE recognizes the new transaction class. - Refactored admin-key extraction in `transactions.service.ts` to dispatch through `TransactionFactory.getNewKeys()`. Adding future transaction types with admin keys is now just a model + factory entry — no service edit needed. ## Manual verification - Personal mode → testnet → returns `UNAUTHORIZED 157`. Expected: the payer is not a governance account, so testnet rejects the create. End-to-end pipeline confirmed working. ## Screenshots 1. Menu for the `Registered Node Create` 2. `Registered Node Create` page

The changes improve clarity in logging, fix join conditions in SQL queries, extend signature key handling, and make the `nodeAccountId` field in the `CachedNode` entity nullable.

[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7357f4fd39efd64d72d32b17f1b8771fdba6f263119d90c6285c1d9a0a52d215L21-R23) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1e19e9f2ede194537478c1e9fa88f639150372f5b0d26d43392a8329fba65ca3R1-R14)

[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6ef3203e473e7f511c85f121e5a48d3cbce4045d524c40567957113f0194199fL271-R271) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-6ef3203e473e7f511c85f121e5a48d3cbce4045d524c40567957113f0194199fL302-R302)

items.title: fix: Gettransactionnodes showing irrelevant transactions
items.body:
**Summary**

  - Users were seeing transactions in their signing queue that they didn't actually need to sign. This happened because the `TransactionCachedAccount` link table didn't distinguish between sender/signer accounts and receiver accounts — so if a user's key appeared on a receiving account of a transfer, the transaction was incorrectly surfaced.
  - Added an `isReceiver` boolean column to `TransactionCachedAccount` to track whether a linked account is the receiver of the transaction.
  - Updated the `getTransactionNodesQuery` SQL to exclude receiver-only account links (`isReceiver = false` filter) in the cached-account signer branch, so transactions only appear for users who actually need to sign.
  - Terminal-status transactions (EXECUTED, FAILED, etc.) bypass the receiver filter so they remain visible in historical views.

**What changed**

  Database & Entity:
  - New migration `TransactionCachedAccountIsReceiver` adds `isReceiver` boolean column (default false) to `transaction_cached_account` table.
  - `TransactionCachedAccount` entity updated with the new column.

  Account caching (`AccountCacheService`):
  - `getAccountInfoForTransaction`, `saveAccountData`, and related methods now accept and propagate an isReceiver flag when linking transactions to cached accounts.
  - `linkTransactionToAccount` inlined (removed generic `cache.helper.ts` dependency) and sets `isReceiver` on insert.

  SQL query (`transaction.queries.ts`):
  - Cached-account signer branch now filters with `AND ta.isReceiver = false`, preventing receiver-only accounts from qualifying a user as a signer.
  - `onlyUnsigned` logic refactored from correlated `NOT EXISTS `subqueries to more efficient `LEFT JOIN ... IS NULL `pattern.
  - `buildWhereClause` simplified: `onlyUnsigned` moved into Roles, user/roles combined into a single `userContext` parameter, `addParam` exposed in return for downstream reuse.
  - Approver CTE changed from `COUNT(*) > 0` to `EXISTS` for

# Expand E2E and Renderer Test Coverage

## Summary

Expands UI automation coverage across the Hedera Transaction Tool by adding new Playwright scenarios, moving suitable UI checks into renderer component tests, refining the test infrastructure (per-describe parallel mode, JSON reporter, `.env` loading at config time, renderer test-hook escape hatch), and refreshing the release coverage matrix in `docs/test-scenarios.md`.

This branch changes **130 files** with **10,095 additions** and **2,903 deletions** across 36 commits.

## What changed

- **Notification preferences** (`@organization-basic`): Ready to Execute, Signature Required, Cancelled toggles persist state changes (3.6.2-4 + 12.2.1-4).
- **Drafts and history table pagination** (`@local-transactions`): drafts table paginates at 10/page (4.3.3) and history table paginates at 10/page (4.4.4). Both tests seed records via the new `seedTransactionRecords` DB helper to avoid building 11+ rows through the UI.
- **Creator-cancels in-progress transaction** (`@organization-advanced`, 6.3.7): asserts a transaction creator can cancel a transaction while it is still in progress.

Update all imports and references to match the correct namespace if the package name changed.

Any namespace changes are reflected consistently throughout the codebase.
Any required proto dependency updates are applied and verified.

items.title: Hide "external" badge from non admin users
fix: Do not broadcast cache refresh notifications when no data changed
items.body:
Fixes #2207 

**Problem**
The MirrorNode account endpoint uses very short-lived **etags** (~2 seconds). Because the cache refresh interval exceeds this window, every scheduled refresh receives an `HTTP 200` with a new **etag** rather than a `304 Not Modified `— even when the underlying account data is identical. The existing code treated every HTTP 200 as a data change, returning `REFRESHED` status and broadcasting a notification to all connected clients. This caused unnecessary client reloads and wasted network traffic on every refresh cycle, regardless of whether anything actually changed.

The node endpoint did not have this problem in practice (its **etags** are longer-lived and 304s work as expected), but it had the same architectural gap — no data-level comparison as a fallback when **etags** fail to suppress a refresh.

**Solution**
Added a data-level comparison layer in both `AccountCacheService` and `NodeCacheService` that runs after a successful `HTTP 200` fetch but before declaring the data as "refreshed":
- **hasAccountDataChanged(fetchedData, cached)** — compares the serialized key (via Buffer.compare) and receiverSignatureRequired field against the previously cached values.
 - **hasNodeDataChanged(fetchedData, cached)** — compares the serialized admin key and nodeAccountId against the previously cached values.

When the fetched data is identical to what was already cached, the service now returns `DATA_UNCHANGED` instead of `REFRESHED`. The `public refreshAccount()` / `refreshNode()` methods only return true for `REFRESHED`, so the upstream `CacheManagementService` correctly skips the notification when data hasn't changed.

A new `DATA_UNCHANGED` enum value was added to `RefreshStatus` to distinguish this case from `NOT_MODIFIED` (which represents an `HTTP 304 response`). The cache is still updated (timestamps, etag, claim release) in both cases — only the client notification is suppressed.

items.title: fix: Add logging framework & format to frontend
items.body:
## Add structured logging framework to the frontend

Fixes #2414 

### Problem

The frontend does not log anything. When users encounter problems there is no diagnostic trail they can share with developers, and developers cannot reproduce issues without guesswork. Every `console.log` scattered across the codebase is silently swallowed in production builds, so errors in websocket connections, transaction signing, database migrations, IPC calls, and version checks vanish without a trace.

### Why `electron-log` instead of `log4js`

The issue suggested evaluating `log4js` first. After investigation, `electron-log` was chosen because:

1. **Already available** — `electron-log` is a dependency of `electron-updater` (which the app already uses for auto-updates), so it introduces zero additional package overhead.
2. **Electron-native** — It understands Electron's process model out of the box (main, renderer, preload), handles `app.getPath('userData')` automatically, and integrates with Electron's crash reporter.
3. **Built-in rotation** — File rotation with configurable max size and archive count is included without additional plugins.
4. **Simpler IPC** — Renderer logs can be forwarded to the main process file transport through the existing preload bridge pattern, without needing a custom transport adapter that `log4js` would require in an Electron context.

### Solution

A structured JSON logging framework that writes to a single rotating log file with automatic sensitive-data sanitization.

**Architecture:**
- **Shared layer** (`shared/utils/logging.ts`) — log level types, argument normalization, key-based redaction, pattern scrubbing (JWT, Bearer, PEM), payload summarization, circular-reference detection, and depth-limited serialization.
- **Main process** (`main/modules/logger.ts`) — configures `electron-log` with a JSON file transport, log rotation (5 MB × 5 archives), console patching, and unhandled error capture. Exposes `createLogger(compone

items.title: refactor: split tests to optimize workers
items.body:
Description:
This PR restructures the Playwright automation suites to improve file-level parallelism and reduce shared E2E wall-clock time without changing the current sequential execution model inside each test file.

Move functional tests into suite-specific folders for local-basic, local-transactions, organization-basic, and organization-advanced
Split local transaction coverage into smaller focused files for transaction execution, drafts, workflow flows, and workflow history/details
Split advanced organization transaction coverage into focused files for status/signing, observer visibility, execution flows, and compatibility scenarios
Keep the existing shared bootstrap and fixture helpers, but apply them across the new suite layout
Increase shared frontend CI worker count from 3 to 4 to take advantage of the new file distribution
Update automation README to reflect the new suite layout, commands, and Playwright worker model
Related issue(s):
Fixes https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2599

Notes for reviewer:
The main behavioral change is scheduling. Large suites are now distributed across more files so Playwright can keep more workers busy during shared runs, while tests inside each file still execute sequentially. Coverage is being redistributed rather than fundamentally changed.

Checklist

 Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
 Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

items.title: feat: use pnpm workspaces
items.body:
Fixes: [2751](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2751)

# Summary

The repo had three independent pnpm workspaces: `back-end/`, `front-end/`, `automation/`. Each shipped its own `package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

This caused four real problems:

- **Dependabot triplication** — every shared-dep bump opened 3 PRs.
- **Version drift** — shared deps could diverge silently between modules.
- **Slow CI** — three `pnpm install` calls per pipeline.
- **Cognitive overhead** — contributors had to know which directory to install in.

Goal: one root workspace, one lockfile, one catalog of shared versions.

# What changed

## Root workspace + catalog

- New `pnpm-workspace.yaml` at the repo root declares all packages.
- New root `package.json` holds a single dependency catalog.
- Child `package.json` files reference the catalog:
  ```json
  "axios": "catalog:"

- Deleted three child lockfiles and the old `back-end/pnpm-workspace.yaml.`
- Result: pnpm install at the root installs everything. `pnpm -F <name> <script>` runs scripts in any child.

## Dependabot consolidation
- `.github/dependabot.yml:` 4 ecosystems → 1 npm ecosystem at the root + github-actions.
- Added groups (hiero-ledger, nestjs, testing) so related bumps land in a single PR.
- This is the direct fix for the issue.

## CI workflows
Updated 4 workflows (test-backend, test-frontend, test-migrations, publish-docker-images):

- Install at the repo root, not per-module.
- Cache against the root `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
- Run scripts via `pnpm -F <module> <script>.`
- Docker build context flipped to `.` (repo root).

## Dockerfiles
Rewrote 4 Dockerfiles (api, chain, notifications, typeorm) on a consistent recipe:

- Build context is the repo root.
- Copy all workspace manifests + the root lockfile before install (keeps the install layer cacheable).
- Filtered install: `pnpm install --filter "back-end..." --filter "@back-end/api..."` — only what each ser

items.title: fix: 2534 duplicate notifications from chain
items.body:
Fixes [#2518](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2518)

## Description

When multiple `chain` pods run in parallel, each pod runs the same cron
jobs on the same schedule. When a transaction expires or changes status,
all pods independently detect it and each publishes a notification event
to NATS. The notifications service processes all of them, resulting in
duplicate emails — one per pod.

## How it was reproduced

Locally with `docker compose up --scale chain=3`. The logs showed all 3
pods publishing the same status-update event:

chain-1 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 2
chain-2 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 3
chain-3 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 4
notifications-1 | Processing email batch... with 3 notifications in 1 groups

## Solution

Database-centric deduplication — no new dependencies (no Redis locks).

Every database UPDATE that precedes a notification emission now includes
a WHERE clause that checks the current status of the row:

```sql
UPDATE transaction
SET status = 'EXPIRED'
WHERE id = :id AND status IN ('NEW', 'WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES', ...)
RETURNING id;
```

The first pod to execute the UPDATE changes the row and gets it back via
RETURNING. The other pods find 0 matching rows (because the status
already changed) and skip the notification.

## Result after the fix

> chain-1 | Published to: notifications.queue.transaction.status-update, seq: 64
notifications-1 | Processing email batch... with 1 notifications in 1 groups
notifications-1 | Message sent: [7881ccba-...@gmail.com](mailto:7881ccba-...@gmail.com)

items.title: Fixing Flickering Buttons
items.body:
**Problem**
When a user signs a transaction and it moves from one status to another, the action buttons (Sign, Cancel, etc.) in the top-right corner of the Transaction Details page flicker - they appear, disappear, and reappear inconsistently. This happens during status transitions like "Waiting for Signatures" → "Awaiting Execution".

The button flickering is caused by the fullyLoaded flag being reset to false every time the transaction data changes.

1. When you click "Sign", after signing completes, fetchTransaction() is called
2. This updates the organizationTransaction prop
3. A watcher in TransactionDetailsHeader.vue detects this change and immediately sets fullyLoaded = false
4. The visibleButtons computed property checks: if (!fullyLoaded.value) return []; - returning an empty array
5. All buttons disappear
6. The watcher then runs async operations (mirror node API call + backend API call)
7. When those complete, fullyLoaded = true is set
8. Buttons reappear

**Solution**
Buttons change from enabled/disabled state instead of being shown/hidden.

items.title: fix: automatically set notifications read after signing
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request introduces a new bulk update mechanism for notification receivers when transactions are signed, improves backend transaction processing, and simplifies related frontend notification handling. The backend now marks relevant notifications as read in bulk, and the frontend removes redundant notification logic, relying on backend updates.

**Backend enhancements:**

* Added `bulkUpdateNotificationReceivers` method to `SignersService`, enabling efficient bulk marking of notifications as read when transactions are updated.
* Integrated notification updates into the transaction signing flow: notifications are collected and updated alongside transaction bytes, ensuring consistency. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-45a2f3f118a08ffd6b7d3f4cecd2d83e838d56cde6cebdec2b57c3ff51d23d75R277) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-45a2f3f118a08ffd6b7d3f4cecd2d83e838d56cde6cebdec2b57c3ff51d23d75R296) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-45a2f3f118a08ffd6b7d3f4cecd2d83e838d56cde6cebdec2b57c3ff51d23d75R320-R324)
* Comprehensive tests for `bulkUpdateNotificationReceivers`, verifying query execution, parameter handling, and conditional behavior.
* Improved test naming for clarity in bulk update scenarios.

**Frontend simplification:**

* Removed notification store imports and notification marking logic from `TransactionNodeRow.vue`, as notifications are now handled by the backend. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bcd22789c7c11313f2fab68765b804f11f579ade8c0f653c405488084a059e7bL8) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-bcd22789c7c11313f2fab68765b804f11f579ade8c0f653c405488084a059e7bL44) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-bcd22789c7c11313f2fab68765b804f11f579ade8c0f653c405488084a059e7bL189-L209)

**Description**:
This PR adds support for running Playwright against an already running Electron dev instance and for deterministically mocking native file dialogs during E2E tests. It is needed because remote debugging and native dialogs are controlled in the Electron app, not from Playwright alone.

* Add `launch` and `attach` modes to the Electron automation bootstrapper
* Connect to a running Electron app over CDP when attach mode is enabled
* Gate remote debugging and certificate-related Electron switches behind automation env vars
* Add test-only IPC and preload hooks for mocking `showOpenDialog` and `showSaveDialog`
* Replace inline dialog monkey-patching in tests with shared dialog mock helpers
* Update automation docs and example env configuration for both launch and attach flows

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #2472

**Notes for reviewer**:
<!-- Provide logs, performance numbers or screenshots of the new functionality -->

For attach mode, start the app from `front-end/` with:

```bash
PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true ELECTRON_REMOTE_DEBUGGING_PORT=9222 pnpm dev
```

All Electron app-side changes are env-gated and should not affect normal user or production flows unless the automation flags are explicitly enabled.

items.title: fix: Transaction components to handle live updates
items.body:
Fixes #2305 

**Problem**
Every `TRANSACTION_ACTION` WebSocket event triggers a full data refetch on every open transaction page for every connected user, regardless of relevance. This creates unnecessary server load and poor UX. Three root causes are:
1. **Global broadcast bug** (`fan-out.service.ts`): `FanOutService.notifyClients()` ignores the per-user `NotifyClientDto[]` array entirely and calls `this.websocket.notifyClient()` — a global Socket.IO `io.emit()` broadcast to ALL connected clients. The user IDs from the DTOs are discarded.
2. **Empty payload:** The event carries `content: ''` — zero information about what changed, which transaction, or what kind of change occurred.
3. **Blind refetch on frontend**: Every component subscribing to `TRANSACTION_ACTION` calls its full fetch function on every event with no filtering. The `useWebsocketSubscription` composable silently drops the payload (callback signature was `() => void`).

**Result:** If user A signs a transaction, ALL connected users on ALL transaction pages refetch ALL their data, with loading spinners flashing, even if the change is irrelevant to them.

---

**Solution:** Targeted Refetch with Metadata-Based Filtering
Enrich the WebSocket event with transaction/group IDs and event type, route it per-user only, and let each frontend page check relevance before refetching. This eliminates the "everyone refetches everything" problem while maintaining full backward compatibility.

Edge Cases Handled:
 - **Reconnection**: `useWebsocketSubscription` re-subscribes after reconnect via `wsStore.$onAction`. Missing events during disconnection are caught on the next relevant action.
 - **Batcher merging**: Multiple events within 500ms for same user merge content arrays. Parser unions all IDs across array items.
 - **Transaction not loaded**: If no matching IDs in current view → event is silently ignored (no refetch, no error).
 - **Pre-load state**: If a page hasn't finished its initial fetch

Users can select which items they want to import: Once users have done their choice, they can click on `Import` button and let TTv2 perform the import. items.title: fix: Improve cache refresh error handling for offline mirror nodes
items.body:
Fixes #2285

**Problem**
When a mirror node goes offline or becomes unavailable, the cache refresh mechanism blocks processing entirely. A single failing mirror node network stalls refresh operations for all networks, producing verbose and repetitive error logs that obscure actionable information. This means transaction processing can be delayed by infrastructure issues outside the system's control.

**Solution**
Implements a per-network circuit breaker pattern (`MirrorNodeCircuitBreaker` service) that isolates mirror node failures by network, preventing a single unhealthy network from blocking cache refresh for healthy ones.

Key Changes:
- Circuit breaker with three states (`CLOSED` / `OPEN` / `HALF_OPEN`): Tracks consecutive failures per mirror node network. After a configurable failure threshold (default: 3), the circuit opens and skips that network's refresh attempts entirely until a reset timeout elapses (default: 60s), at which point it transitions to half-open and probes with a limited number of attempts before fully recovering.
- Non-blocking, per-network processing: `refreshStaleAccounts() `and `refreshStaleNodes()` now group entities by `mirrorNetwork` and process each group independently. If one network's circuit is open, its entities are skipped while all other networks continue refreshing normally.
- Concurrency guard: A `refreshInProgress` flag prevents overlapping cache refresh executions, avoiding redundant work during slow refreshes.
- Aggregated error logging: Per-entity errors are collected into a `Set` per network and logged once as a single aggregate warning (e.g., `Account refresh failures for network "testnet": timeout; connection refused`), replacing repetitive per-entity error spam. 
- Configurable via environment variables:
   - `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD` (default: 3)
   - `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_RESET_TIMEOUT_MS` (default: 60000)
   - `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_HALF_OPEN_MAX_ATTEMPTS` (default: 1)

---

**Before:**
Mirror nod

items.title: 2112 transaction queries to use caches
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request introduces significant refactoring and improvements to the transaction node querying logic, notification emission, and related testing. The most important changes include replacing legacy transaction node aggregation/filtering with a new SQL-based approach, streamlining notification updates, and updating tests and module dependencies to support these changes.

**Transaction Node Query Refactor:**

* Major refactor of `TransactionNodesService` to use direct SQL queries (via `SqlBuilderService` and `getTransactionNodesForUser`) instead of aggregating and filtering transactions in memory. This improves performance, simplifies logic, and centralizes filtering. (`back-end/apps/api/src/transactions/nodes/transaction-nodes.service.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f04cebaf2c191881eb159393331657994eee18f96f52e87e6eb8c043fe7db6d9L2-R18) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f04cebaf2c191881eb159393331657994eee18f96f52e87e6eb8c043fe7db6d9L31-L254)
* Introduction of `TRANSACTION_STATUS_COLLECTIONS` constant to standardize transaction status groupings used in node queries. (`back-end/apps/api/src/transactions/nodes/transaction-node-collections.constants.ts`)
* Addition of `SqlBuilderModule` to the transactions module dependencies to support the new SQL-based approach. (`back-end/apps/api/src/transactions/transactions.module.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7d803d62fab311bd9d55a16583ce0247dbcc4280dfb64e4ae7098cd15aa37737L23-R23) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7d803d62fab311bd9d55a16583ce0247dbcc4280dfb64e4ae7098cd15aa37737R56)

**Notification Emission Changes:**

* Refactored notification emission for transaction status updates to use a simplified payload (only `entityId`), removing additional data previously sent. This affects both `TransactionGroupsService` and `TransactionsService`. (`back-end/apps/api/src/transactions/groups/transaction-groups.service.ts`, `back-end/apps/api/src/transactions/transactions.service.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6d72cb8fb9bb871d20c95b9

items.title: Messaging layer replacement and improvements
items.body:
**Description**:
RabbitMQ has some limitations due to our desire to maintain a lightweight backend. In order to properly set up RabbitMQ for our messaging needs, it would require a lot more resources. Nats + Jetstream will be used as the replacement. 

With this change, we now will be using a proper at-most-once queue for all messaging that should only be processed by a single replica of a single service. We will also be using a at-least-once fan out type queue for all broadcast communications to enable all connected websockets to push the proper notifications. 

Speed improvements were added as well. Transaction Group signing was a big issue. If the group was too large, and multiple keys were involved, the backend could not keep up. This causes a number of issues, including unresponsive UI, numerous emails/inapp notifications, etc. Now signing is done in a batched manner, all calls to the database are grouped were possible. 

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #1740 #1800 #1887 #1625 #1770 #1929 #1965 #1906

**Notes for reviewer**:
<!-- Provide logs, performance numbers or screenshots of the new functionality -->

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

This pull request introduces several improvements and refactors to the transaction groups API, focusing on DTO mapping, controller flexibility, and service logic/testing. The main changes include enhanced DTO transformation and validation, support for partial/full group fetches via a new query parameter, and expanded test coverage for the service layer.

**DTO and Mapping Improvements:**

* Added a `group` property to `TransactionGroupItemDto`, with proper type transformation to `TransactionGroupDto` for improved nested DTO mapping. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-650b9957ee7aa875141eb42ed577ebd267095dca6b371c424f9ba4cb18639403R1-R55) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c3a2d9d0b4552ace368123371122f4821aa8e2cc2778d688191e6a2f912f7da0L31-R83)

items.title: Fix fail-open signature validation when mirror node key resolution fails
items.body:
**Description:**
`hasValidSignatureKey` (`back-end/libs/common/src/utils/sdk/key.ts:31-46`) evaluates `0 >= (key.threshold || keys.length)` when given an empty `KeyList`, returning `true` — treating an unknown signing requirement as satisfied. The empty list originates in `TransactionSignatureService.computeSignatureKey` (`back-end/libs/common/src/transaction-signature/transaction-signature.service.ts:38-62`), where every `add*Key` helper silently swallows mirror-node fetch failures and pushes nothing. Any mirror hiccup during status evaluation causes under-signed transactions to advance to `WAITING_FOR_EXECUTION`, get submitted, be rejected with `INVALID_SIGNATURE`, and land in terminal `FAILED` — permanently consuming the TransactionId and forcing a full rebuild.

**Acceptance Tests:**
- `hasValidSignatureKey` returns `false` for an empty `KeyList` (zero threshold, zero children)
- Each `add*Key` helper propagates mirror fetch errors rather than catching and discarding them
- `processTransactionStatus` keeps a transaction in `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES` when required-key resolution throws, rather than advancing it
- `getValidatedSDKTransaction` (`back-end/libs/common/src/execute/execute.service.ts:219`) behaves the same way under key-resolution failure
- Unit tests cover: empty key list, partial mirror failure (some helpers succeed, one throws), and full mirror outage

**Notes:**
Consider emitting a metric/alert on required-key resolution failure rate so mirror degradation is visible before it manifests as failed governance transactions.

- Field is populated correctly for both direct signing and signature import flows
- Existing transaction_signer records without this field do not break on read

items.title: Audit & harden API-uploaded signature flow across organization-advanced tests
items.body:
## Summary

`signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi` bypasses the UI signing flow and uploads signatures directly to `POST /transactions/signers`. This is faster, but it can race with an in-flight UI creator sign.

The backend updates `transactionBytes` using a read–modify–write flow per signature upload. If the creator signs in the UI while the helper uploads signatures, some signatures can be dropped from the transaction bytes.

`TransactionSigner` rows are still created, so the helper appears successful, but the transaction never reaches the required signature threshold and remains stuck in `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES`.

As a result:
- transactions never appear in **Ready for Execution**
- tests intermittently time out
- failures are more common under parallel Playwright load

# Goal

Make all org-advanced tests using `signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi` race-free and document the helper contract clearly.

# Root Cause

The helper uploads signatures directly through the API while some tests still allow the creator to sign through the UI.

This creates a race condition because:
1. the backend performs read–modify–write updates on `transactionBytes`
2. concurrent uploads overwrite each other
3. signer rows are inserted successfully
4. transaction bytes may still miss required signatures

`processTransactionStatus` reads signer data from `transactionBytes`, not from `TransactionSigner` rows. If signatures are missing from the bytes, the transaction remains in `WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURES`.

# In Scope

- `organizationTransactionTests.test.ts`
- `organizationTransactionExecutionTests.test.ts`
- `organizationRegressionTests.test.ts`
- Any caller of:
  - `signTxByAllUsersAndRefresh`
  - `ensureComplexFileExists`

that relies on a UI creator-sign before the helper.

# Acceptance Criteria

No caller of `signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi` is preceded by an un-awaited UI creator-sign.

Callers must either:
- set `isSignRequiredFromCreator=false` and let the helper sign all users, or
- explicit

items.title: Investigate stale “signature required” notifications after execution
items.body:
**Description**:  
Users report seeing notifications that still indicate signatures are required even after the transaction has already been executed, and they never signed the transaction. In the database, the `notification_receiver` entities for that user/notification/type are already gone, suggesting the notification was cleared server-side but the UI did not update. There may be an issue where a status-change event (e.g., to `EXECUTED`) was not sent or not handled correctly, even though there are no stuck messages in NATS.

**Requirements**:  
- Investigate the notification lifecycle for transactions moving to `EXECUTED`, especially when the user has not signed.  
- Verify that:
  - The backend emits the appropriate notification-clear / status-update event when a transaction executes.  
  - The client subscribes to and correctly handles that event to remove or update the notification.    

**Notes**:
I do not know if a hard restart clears the notifications, nor how long the app was open before use. It could be a websocket issue, where the user received the notification, slept the machine long enough for the notifications to come and and clear NATS, then reopened the machine and now notifications didn't repull/auto clear.
