items.title: Missing transactions for newly connected users
items.body:
## Ready to Sign Tab Not Loading Transactions on First Load

### Description
When a new user sets up the transaction tool and connects to an organization for the first time, pending transactions that were already sent to them do not appear in the "Ready to Sign" tab until the user navigates away and returns to the tab.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a transaction and send it to all users who need to sign
2. One of the required signers has not yet set up the transaction tool
3. User sets up the transaction tool and connects to the organization
4. User launches the app, auto navigating to the "Ready to Sign" tab
5. User navigates to a different tab/page
6. User returns to "Ready to Sign" tab
7. Transactions now appear correctly

### Expected Behavior
The "Ready to Sign" tab should display all pending transactions that require the user's signature immediately upon first load after connecting to the organization.

### Actual Behavior
Pending transactions only appear after the user navigates away from and back to the "Ready to Sign" tab.

### Root Cause Investigation Needed
- [ ] Determine why transaction fetching fails on initial load
- [ ] Check if this is a data loading/initialization issue
- [ ] Verify if organization connection triggers proper data sync
- [ ] Investigate if navigation triggers a refresh that initial load doesn't

### Additional Context
- Issue appears to be specific to first-time setup scenarios
- Navigation-based refresh works as expected
- May indicate a race condition or missing initialization step

items.title: TTv2 importing from TTv1
items.body:
TTv2 needs to support importing transactions, signatures, comments, and zips from TTv1. 

For transactions, TTv2 should automatically try and find the other files that should go with the transaction, like the .txt file. TTv1 should not have signatures stored in the .tx file itself.

For signatures, TTv2 should automatically grab all the signatures (especially if multiple signatures exist due to multi node submission), and collate them to the existing transaction, if it exists. Then the transaction needs to be saved. 

TTv2 needs to be able to handle csv and zip and other type formats that the 'batch' transactions may come in. Thus allowing TTv2 to import all data that is exportable and used currently in TTv1.

items.title: Submitting a freeze-upgrade transaction has a INVALID_FREEZE_TRANSACTION_BODY error
items.body: ### Description When submitting a freeze-upgrade transaction to the network, it will fail with a INVALID_FREEZE_TRANSACTION_BODY error. This happened in testnet today https://hashscan.io/testnet/transaction/1761755405.145732000 ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create freeze transaction 2. Specify type: freeze-upgrade 3. Give file hash 4. Give start time 5. Gather signatures 6. Execute 7. See error ### Additional context _No response_ ### Hedera network testnet ### Version v0.17.0 ### Operating system macOS

items.title: TTv1 and TTv2 Support
items.body:
### Problem

The timeframe for full adoption is very large, due to the nature of the user base. This means that users will be instructed to install and run TTv2 long before it will be used. By the time full adoption is achieved, some users will need to be retrained. 

### Solution

Allow TTv2 to be backwards compatible. Full backwards compatibility (including the use of OneDrive) may not be desired. A proposed solution is as follows:

TTv2 will be used as the main application for creating and managing transactions. The creator of the transaction will use TTv2 to create the transaction and upload it to the back-end, allowing users of TTv2 to sign the transaction normally. The creator will then export the transaction to the OneDrive location, allowing users of TTv1 to sign the transaction in the normal fashion as well. 

The creator will gather the signatures from the OneDrive location, and import them into TTv2. TTv2 import will take the signatures, attach them to the transaction, and update the transaction that is stored in the back-end. The back-end will not have any user data for the signatures, but the signatures themselves will be stored in the transaction. 

TTv2 front end will be able to display which keys were used to sign the transaction, and will execute the transaction at the designated time. TTv1 CLI will no longer be necessary. 

### Alternatives

A possible alternative would be to allow TTv2 to pull the signatures from OneDrive automatically. This should be considered and discussed.

items.title: Sign button doesn't disappear after signing
items.body:
### Problem

When signing a transaction, the sign button doesn't go away even though I got a toast message saying the transaction was signed successfully. This is caused by a connection issue with the WebSocketGateway (e.g. ERROR [WebsocketGateway] Socket client connected without user). 
As this bug allowed for a transition to be signed multiple times, it was noted that each time the transaction was signed, a new transaction was essentially created and submitted to the network. This is likely due to the transaction submitting to the network between the first signature and the second.
Due to multiple signings/transactions, the first transaction was successful, but subsequent transactions were marked as duplicate.

### Solution

The root cause of the issue needs to be addressed, as well as adding safeguards for potentially related issues.
1. Discover why the websocket did not reconnect. This specific case happened due to a user having the app open and running while the notification service pod was redeployed. Websocket should have reconnected, but doesn't appear to have done so properly.
2. Determine if a client side health check and reconnect to the server feature is possible
3. Sign button could be changed to not rely solely on the websocket notification, but also on the response to the signing of the transaction. If a transaction is signed and the server responds with a successful upload, the sign button can be hidden.
4. On the back-end, if the transaction is already being executed, prevent further signatures
5. Ensure that a transaction doesn't get 'duplicated' due to the above.
6. When duplicate transactions are found in history, either display all transactions, or at least display the transaction that is not a duplicate so that history will show that the first transaction was successful. 
7. If a transaction isn't actually duplicated, but instead has its status/response code updated due to the duplicate submission, don't allow the duplicate submission

items.title: Allow users to sort transactions by modified timestamp
items.body:
This issues arises to address the importing of signatures, but may also help the overall user experience. 

When importing signatures, there is not currently a clean and easy way to show the user which transactions were modified AND provide a link for each altered transaction. Something like this should be added in the future. For now, the modified timestamp (updatedAt) should be displayed to the user in the Ready to Sign, In Progress, and Ready for Execution tabs. This will allow the user to sort by last modified and see all transactions that were affected by the import.

This is a temporary solution, but the addition of this sorting column may be desired even once another solution is implemented.

items.title: TTv2 exporting with fixed bytes
items.body:
As the Java SDK and JS SDK don't currently output the same bytes when encoding, the export feature as it currently is won't work. The keys used in Java and JS will not be signing the same bytes. Fixing the JS SDK so it will have the correct output is not really possible as it will likely break other projects. So another solution is needed.

When a transaction has signatures, and is then encoded, the bytes are basically considered 'frozen'. This means that when the transaction is decoded in either Java or JS, the transaction's body bytes will not be reconstructed. This allows the transaction to be created in one SDK, then signed in another, then those signatures collated in the first SDK. 

The solution for this exporting problem is to:
1. Create the transaction in TTv2 
2. When the user 'Exports' the transaction in TTv1 format, the user will sign the transaction before encoding (Note, this signature is not saved and will basically be thrown away)
3. The transaction is imported in TTv1 and signed by users

The only potential issue is if the user does not have any keys, they will not be able to export in TTv1 format.

items.title: Signatures gathered from different versions of the tool can be incorrect
items.body:
Refer to TTv1 issue: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool-demo/issues/578

Even in TTv2, transactions are stored in bytes, and deserialized into a transaction. This means that it relies on the SDK (which uses protobuf). If the different front-end versions use different SDK versions, then one tool will deserialize the transaction bytes differently than another front-end. This causes some signatures to be incorrect.

Ensure that there are some sort of checks to catch this mismatch error.

items.title: 1015 enhance smart collating to check early and often where applicable
items.body:
**Description**:
Smart collating was only occurring at the end. This would cause a transaction to be 'waiting to execute' even if it should be in 'waiting to sign' due to the transaction size and signature key list being too large. Smart collating is now checked early, and often, to ensure the users see the actual state the transaction is in.

In addition, while 'reject' would likely be a better status for the transaction that is unable to go through smart collating successfully, it would require more effort for the front end code. For now, the status used in this case will be 'failed'

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #1015 

**Notes for reviewer**:
updateTransactionStatus functionality has been refactored. Please review this change.

**Checklist**

- [x] Documented (Code comments, README, etc.)
- [x] Tested (unit, integration, etc.)

Pino has been flagged for protest ware. As it hasn't been removed, and doesn't appear that it will be addressed anytime soon, a different logger will be used.

items.title: Add the status to the group transaction detail view
items.body:
### Problem

Add the status to the group transaction detail view. For example if the group transaction was cancelled, it should be shown in the group transaction details view as it shows in history.

### Solution

Add the status to the group transaction detail view.

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Verify Smart Collate properly removes superfluous signatures
items.body:
Issue from TTv1: https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool-demo/issues/575

TTv1 removes one signature at a time until it can create a transaction within the size limitation that also has a valid signature map. 

TTv2 just checks if the signed transaction is too large, then removes all superfluous signatures, prioritizing single keys over threshold keys. This only happens on the transaction being submitted, not the transaction being stored. This process needs to be reviewed to ensure it is properly collating the signatures.

items.title: When reducing the transaction size to stay within the limit, the tool should remove only the signatures that are not required to satisfy any thresholds
items.body:
### Problem

When optimizing a transaction to meet the size limit, the tool should remove extra signatures only from threshold keys that have already been satisfied. It must not remove signatures from threshold keys that are still incomplete, as doing so could prevent the transaction from meeting required signing conditions.

Once the signatures are removed, the tool should verify all the thresholds have been one then prepare to send the transaction.


### Solution

Update the feature to support this.

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: TTv2 exporting of transactions
items.body:
TTv2 needs to support exporting of transactions. This includes single and group transactions. 

For single transactions: the exported data will need to include the body bytes and signature bytes stored in the transaction, as well as the comments that are app specific. 

For group transactions: the exported data will need to include the body bytes, signature bytes, app specific comments for each transaction, as well as the group transaction properties.

items.title: Transaction verify after collate
items.body: In TTv2, after collating and preparing a transaction for submittal, perform another transaction check to ensure signatures are valid and meet threshold requirement of key objects.  This will ensure the collate function has properly attached signatures and the transsction is valid for processing before submitting.

items.title: Allow manual transaction submission
items.body:
### Problem

A user may want to create a transaction, which will be submitted to the network manually.
The transaction will still have the same stages, waiting for signatures and waiting for execution but the submission should be manual.
The creator will have the option to cancel, archive it, and submit it if waiting for execution,

### Solution

- The transaction has to be marked as "submit manually" from the current "Is sign-only" check box. (change it)
- remove the mark-sign-only
- add a flag in the transaction entity whether it is a manual transaction
- disable the automatic execution of the transaction if the manual flag is true
- only manual transactions can be archived

In the waiting-for-signatures status there should be the following:
- for the creator: Sign (if required), Cancel, Archive, Export
- for the signer: Sign, Export

The transaction should be shown in the In Progress for both creator and signer if they have signed it

In the waiting-for-execution status there should be the following:
- for the creator: Submit, Cancel, Archive, Export
- for the signer: Export

The corn jobs for the status transition from:
- waiting-for-signatures to waiting-for-execution
- waiting-for-signatures to expired
- waiting-for-execution to expired

should still exist

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Provide notification for transaction creators
items.body:
### **User Story**

As a transaction creator, I want to be notified when a transaction has received its signatures, executes, or fails so I can take the appropriate action to manage my transactions.



### **Acceptance Criteria**
**Notification for Signatures Received:**

The transaction creator/observer receives a notification when the transaction has received the minimum number of signatures required for execution.
The notification message should state: Transactions ready for execution.
The notification should include a link to view the transaction details in the Ready for Execution status.

**Notification for Successful Execution:**

The transaction creator/observer receives a notification when the transaction is successfully executed on the network.
The notification message should state: Transaction executed successfully.
The notification should link to the history page when the user clicks on it.

**Notification for Transaction Failure:**

The transaction creator/observer receives a notification if the transaction fails upon execution.
The notification message should state: Transaction failed.
Clicking on the notification should navigate the user to the transaction history with details about the failure.
The new entires in history should have a badge. 
The badge is removed when the user navigates away from the history page. 

**Notification for Transaction Expiration**

The transaction creator/observer receives a notification if the transaction expires before receiving the required number of signatures.
The notification message should state: Transaction expired.
The notification should include a link to view the expired transaction details in the history tab when clicked.

**Notification Delivery**
Notifications should also be sent via email service.

**Notification Management**

The transaction creator/observer should be able to view all notifications in the notifications center.
The transaction creator/observer should be able to dismiss or mark notifications

In this case maybe it should be "Sign and Share"

If I am a creator of the transaction, but my signature is not required on the transaction then something like "Create and Share" would make sense.

### Solution

items.title: Provide notifications for transaction signers and observers
items.body:
### **User Story**
As a transaction signer, I want to easily navigate to the organization and network where the transaction I need to review and sign is located when it's prepared for execution.


As a transaction observer, I want to know about the different stages a transaction goes through so I can stay up to date on its progress. 

### Acceptance Criteria

Note: Each time a user clicks on the notification type the application should take them to the right transaction status or history page in the right organization and network. 

**Notification for Successful Execution:**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification when the transaction is successfully executed on the network.
The notification message should state: Transaction executed successfully.
The notification should link to the history page when the user clicks on it.

**Notification for Transaction Failure:**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification if the transaction fails upon execution.
The notification message should state: Transaction failed.
Clicking on the notification should navigate the user to the transaction history with details about the failure.
The new entires in history should have a badge. 
The badge is removed when the user navigates away from the history page. 

**Notification for Transaction Expiration**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification if the transaction expires before receiving the required number of signatures.
The notification message should state: Transaction expired.
The notification should include a link to view the expired transaction details in the history tab when clicked.

**Notification for Transaction approval**

The transaction signer/observer receives a notification if the transaction is approved by an approver. 
The notification message should state: Transaction approved [user].
The notification should include a link to view the expired transaction details in the history tab when clicked.

**Notification for Transaction

items.title: When we look at any transactions that update an entity we should highlight which properties changed from before to after
items.body:
### Problem

When I look at the details button of an account update transaction that executed I see the Account Changes section could be enhanced to note what property was modified in this transaction.

When I look at these details:

![Screenshot 2024-05-28 at 6 08 24 PM](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/assets/21989138/96d5c4b9-4ae4-4639-b737-57ceab7532f5)

It clear to me that:
- The key did not change as it says none
- the memo did get updates in this transaction to test. We should also include what it was before this change.
- Staking did not change because it says none
- Decline staking rewards I cannot tell if it was changed in this transaction from yes to no or if it was previously also no
- Receiver signature required - i can't tell if this changed from yes to no or if this was previously no
- Max automatic associations - I cannot tell if this changed to 0 or was previously zero

What is shown for each property should be consistent. We are using the word "None" in some places to indicate that those properties had no change while in others we are using "No" or "0" so hard to tell if those had no change or were changed to those new values in this transaction. If there is a property that was changed in this transaction we should show what was the previous state before this update. 






### Solution

See above 

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: investigate proper signing method in a multi signature flow
items.body:
If a signature is added to a transaction, any other signers that sign that transaction will then have a signature that is only valid with the transaction that has the previously created signatures. This means that all signers should sign the base transaction.

If the above is true, then further investigation is required to ensure that the signatures that are created will pass verification when submitted.

items.title: Notarization Failure
items.body: The application failed the notarization process due to missing a usable signature or having resource issues (such as no resources being present when the signature indicates they must be).

items.title: investigate multi-node signing solution
items.body:
The SDK function for addSignature doesn't work in a multi-node situation. But adding the same signature to each sub transaction (where a sub transaction is the same as the main transaction but has the node ID specified by the sdk) shouldn't work either as the node ID is part of the transaction that is signed. 

Some more research needs to be done to properly handle the adding, and possible removing, of signatures from a transaction that has multiple nodes.

items.title: Improper Code Signing
items.body: The application is either not signed with a valid Apple Developer ID or is signed with an ad-hoc signature.

items.title: Test creating, signing and executing an account with 100+ keys
items.body:
### Problem

1. Generate ~100 keys
2. Create an account that has this key structure + 5 additional thresholds https://hashscan.io/mainnet/adminKey/0.0.58
3. Update the property of the account and sign with all of the keys on the account
4. Execute the transaction

Transactions have a transaction size limit (6,144-byte). If the transaction size limit is exceeded because of the number of signatures applied to the transaction, we have randomly remove some of the non-required signatures upon execution so that the transaction can successfully be submitted at the valid start time. 

### Solution

Test the above scenario. Implement a solution that will check the transaction size limit before execution and smartly remove some of the non-required signatures from the transaction. 

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Send email notifications when the transaction has received enough signatures
items.body:
### Problem

User Story:

As a transaction creator, I need to know when the transactions have received enough signatures to execute successfully. 

When a transaction has received the minimum number of signatures, send an email notification to the creator. 
The email should include which users have signed and which signers are outstanding potentially.

### Solution

See above 

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Add Smart Collating
items.body:
### Problem

When users sign a transaction, the signatures are added to the transaction itself. The actual transaction should not have any signatures added until it is ready to submit. 

In addition, if more signatures are gathered than needed, and the resulting signed transactions is larger than the transactions size limit, then only the minimum signatures should be added.

### Solution

After every user signature upload:
 1. Collate the gathered signatures to determine if the transaction has the require signatures.
 2. Ensure the resulting transaction with the collated signatures doesn't result in a length over the limit.
 3. Save the valid result to  'collated body'

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Add signature bytes to the Transaction-Signer entity
items.body:
### Problem

The transaction signer entity is missing the signature property. Signatures created on the front end cannot be saved for future use (smart collating).

### Solution

For smart collating to work, signatures need to be added and removed until a valid transaction is obtained. Transactions cannot currently remove signatures. This means that the current solution for this issue is to store the signatures in the database in order to be used as needed during collation against a fresh copy of the transaction.

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: Account Update Transaction page "payer" dropdown doesn't include all my accounts
items.body:
### Description

I have an account on testnet. I created a transaction to create a new account. This worked. Then I went to update the account, and the "Payer ID" drop down only listed my original account, not the new one. When I switch from one organization to another, and then try to create an update account transaction, I see both accounts in the dropdown.

Another thing is that the Account ID field would be nice to have as an editable dropdown, so I could select an account from there. Otherwise, I have to leave the screen to go look at my accounts and copy the account ID.

In addition, if I select an existing account, all the remaining fields should populate with the existing values for that account (memo, staking rewards, etc) so only those things I change will be updated.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8da13daf-fb43-490c-a065-07ceae42ec28)


### Steps to reproduce

### Additional context

v0.4.1

### Operating system

items.title: Enhance smart collating to check early and often where applicable
items.body:
### Problem

When a transaction is signed, and the signature uploaded, the server attempts to determine the status of the transaction. If the transaction has a valid signature, the status is set to 'Ready to execute'. The users will see the transaction as ready, even if the transaction is too large and will not pass the smart collating. There needs to be more checks for the transaction size issue.

### Solution

When a signature is uploaded and the update status event is emitted:

// Check the current status of the transaction
if (status == 'ready to execute') done, no further action is required
else if (status == 'ready to sign')
  //determine if the transaction is now valid after the update
  if (!hasValidSignatureKey) done, no further action is required
  else
    //Check the size of the transaction, including signatures
    if (transaction.size <= max) update transaction status to 'ready to execute' and emit event
    else
      //attempt smart collate to determine a valid signature key can be created
      if (smartCollate == success) update transaction status to 'ready to execute' and emit event
      else done, no further action required.

The concept is to add the check on size into the update status function in order to more quickly inform the users if their signatures are still required because an actual valid key has not yet been met.

### Alternatives

_No response_

items.title: PoC Notification Controls
items.body:
With the Notifications (both email and in-app) we will need to provide users with the functionality to control (enable/disable) the notifications they wish to receive. For the PoC we can introduce one additional section in Settings page, which will be "Notifications". From there the user will be able to Enable or Disable the initial 2 types of Email notifications we are introducing:
- Transaction Threshold Reached / (as additional info text we can add) "You will be notified when a transaction you are a Signer in has collected enough signature to satisfy the threshold" - Enable/Disable toggle 
- Required Signature / Info text - "You will be notified whenever a transaction, which requires your signature has been created" - Enable/Disable toggle

items.title: fix: mark notifications as read when navigating
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request enhances how transaction and transaction group notification indicators are handled in the front-end. Now, when a user views the details of a transaction or a transaction group, any related "sign" notification indicators are automatically marked as read. This improves the user experience by ensuring notification badges are cleared promptly and accurately. The changes also refactor and centralize logic in the notifications store to better filter and access organization-specific notifications.

**Transaction and Notification Handling Improvements:**

* In both `TransactionDetails.vue` and `TransactionGroupDetails.vue`, added logic to filter for "sign" notification indicators related to the currently viewed transaction or group and mark them as read when the details are loaded. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c401ef22a0144c34436962f52b4560dbf28ffc9522005aa0ef73ddf6e77bb881R180-R192) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c05cf707192551ffcae3bfb5043671e58bc03b5ade99174069cb8653d2363354R585-R599)
* Added imports and usage of the notifications store (`useNotificationsStore`) in both detail views to support this new behavior. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c401ef22a0144c34436962f52b4560dbf28ffc9522005aa0ef73ddf6e77bb881R54-R60) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c05cf707192551ffcae3bfb5043671e58bc03b5ade99174069cb8653d2363354R74) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c05cf707192551ffcae3bfb5043671e58bc03b5ade99174069cb8653d2363354R100)

**Notifications Store Refactoring:**

* Added `currentOrganizationNotifications` and related computed properties to `storeNotifications.ts` to provide easy access to notifications relevant to the currently selected organization and network.
* Updated notification marking methods (`markAsRead`, `markAsReadIds`) to use the new computed key for consistency and correctness. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a2317247ad71eb6c043d51bffd93a747660aad0acea4331557c53f8a85dcff1cL169-R183) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a2317247ad71eb6c043d51bffd93a747660aad0acea4331557c53f8a85dcff1cL19

items.title: feat: extend automation coverage
items.body:
# Expand E2E and Renderer Test Coverage

## Summary

Expands UI automation coverage across the Hedera Transaction Tool by adding new Playwright scenarios, moving suitable UI checks into renderer component tests, refining the test infrastructure (per-describe parallel mode, JSON reporter, `.env` loading at config time, renderer test-hook escape hatch), and refreshing the release coverage matrix in `docs/test-scenarios.md`.

This branch changes **130 files** with **10,095 additions** and **2,903 deletions** across 36 commits.

## What changed

### New automated coverage

- **Transaction form validation** (`@local-basic`): transfer (5.5.7-5.5.11), header (5.14.6-5.14.8), file-ID (5.8.6/5.8.7/5.9.6/5.9.7/14.5.2), file-create owner-key (5.7.7), allowance owner/spender (5.6.5/5.6.6), complex-key duplicate (5.13.6).
- **Account ID checksum** on Link Existing Account (14.5.1) appended to `workflowTests.test.ts` (`@local-transactions` — needs mirror node).
- **Settings → Organizations tab** (`@local-basic`): table headers (3.4.4), connection status badge for connected / disconnected / upgradeRequired (3.4.5), version info badges (3.4.6), disconnect-reason note (13.2.3), blocked org selection (13.2.4), global Update Available modal (15.1.1).
- **Notification preferences** (`@organization-basic`): Ready to Execute, Signature Required, Cancelled toggles persist state changes (3.6.2-4 + 12.2.1-4).
- **Drafts and history table pagination** (`@local-transactions`): drafts table paginates at 10/page (4.3.3) and history table paginates at 10/page (4.4.4). Both tests seed records via the new `seedTransactionRecords` DB helper to avoid building 11+ rows through the UI.
- **Creator-cancels in-progress transaction** (`@organization-advanced`, 6.3.7): asserts a transaction creator can cancel a transaction while it is still in progress.
- Earlier in the branch: account reset, login/session, settings, keys, files, transaction drafts/files, account create/update/delete/dat

items.title: fix: restore organization notification automation suite
items.body:
**Description**:                                                                                                                                                                                        
  This PR restores the organization in-app notification automation suite (12.1.1–12.1.5) that was previously skipped, fixes the underlying renderer bug that made the suite flaky, and extends coverage with a new real-time tab-badge test (12.1.6). It also splits `automation/utils/db/databaseQueries.ts` into three thematic modules to improve maintainability.                                                                                                                                                             
                       
  * Fix `storeNotifications` not refetching on user switch within a single organization (root cause of the failing notification suite); add a watcher on the composite         
  `serverUrl|userId` key and clear the org's slice on logout
  * Re-enable five previously skipped in-app notification tests (12.1.1–12.1.5)                                                                                                
  * Add new test `Verify Ready to Sign tab badge count tracks notifications as transactions are viewed` (12.1.6) that exercises the badge count flow `0 → 1 → 2 → 1 → 0`       
  * Add `OrganizationPage.getReadyToSignTabBadgeCount()` and `createAdditionalNotificationForUser()` helpers to support the new badge-count assertions                         
  * Split `automation/utils/db/databaseQueries.ts` into `localQueries.ts` (SQLite), `userQueries.ts` (Postgres user/key/admin), and `notificationQueries.ts` (Postgres         
  notifications); the original file becomes a barrel so existing imports keep working                                                                                          
  * Add JSDoc to every exported query helper; drop misleading `@throws` tags from helpers that catch internally

items.title: Showing the freeze type on the transaction list table
items.body: **Problem** To help move faster during releases and in the narrow time window for abort transactions, the Transaction `Freeze` type needs to be added in the` Transaction Type` column of the transaction list table. **Solution** The backend does not store the specific freeze transaction type (Freeze Only, Prepare Upgrade, Freeze Upgrade, Freeze Abort) - it only stores the generic "FREEZE" type in the database. The freeze subtype information is only available within the serialized transactionBytes. To minimize the impact on the codebase and avoid backend changes, a **frontend-only approach** was implemented: 1. New utility function `getFreezeTypeForTransaction() `in **transactions.ts**: * When the frontend detects a freeze transaction, it makes an additional API call to `getTransactionById()` to fetch the full transaction details including transactionBytes * Deserializes the transaction bytes using the Hedera SDK * Extracts the `freezeType` code from the FreezeTransaction instance * Results are cached in a Map to avoid redundant API calls for the same transaction 2. New utility function `getDisplayTransactionType() `in **transactions.ts**: * Accepts either an SDK Transaction or Uint8Array * For freeze transactions, extracts and returns the specific freeze type string * Falls back to standard transaction type for non-freeze transactions 3. Updated components: * **TransactionNodeRow.vue** (Ready to Sign, In Progress, Ready for Execution, History tabs in organization mode): Added a watcher that detects freeze transactions and fetches the freeze type on-demand * **History.vue** (personal mode): Uses the existing body field from local transactions to extract freeze type * **Drafts.vue**: Parses the transactionBytes field to display specific freeze types for draft transactions **Before**

items.title: fix: automatically set notifications read after signing
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request introduces a new bulk update mechanism for notification receivers when transactions are signed, improves backend transaction processing, and simplifies related frontend notification handling. The backend now marks relevant notifications as read in bulk, and the frontend removes redundant notification logic, relying on backend updates.

**Backend enhancements:**

* Added `bulkUpdateNotificationReceivers` method to `SignersService`, enabling efficient bulk marking of notifications as read when transactions are updated.
* Integrated notification updates into the transaction signing flow: notifications are collected and updated alongside transaction bytes, ensuring consistency. **Frontend simplification:**

items.title: Improve connection status UX and resilience
items.body:
**Description:**
The app currently surfaces vague errors like "organization is unreachable" with no meaningful feedback during connection attempts. Connection status needs to be clearly communicated per-org, reconnection logic needs to be reliable, and the UI should reflect real-time state throughout.

**Acceptance Tests:**
- On launch, each org displays a visible "connecting…" indicator while a connection is being established
- Selecting an unreachable org shows a cancelable overlay modal with live connection status (e.g. "Trying to establish connection…")
- If a cooldown exists between retries, the overlay shows a countdown: "Couldn't connect — retrying in 10s"
- Once connected, the overlay dismisses automatically
- Canceling the modal returns the user to org selection without leaving them in a broken state
- Failed connection attempts are logged locally with timestamps for diagnosis
- Reconnection attempts continue correctly in the background without manual intervention

**Notes:**
Local logs should capture enough detail to diagnose intermittent failures (timestamp, org, attempt count, error) without exposing sensitive data.

items.title: Transaction Approval Groups & Workflows
items.body:
The proposed features introduce predefined approval groups, approver workflows, conditional transaction visibility, and clearer status indicators to streamline operations, reduce human error, and enhance security for organizations managing Hedera entities.

## **Background and Motivation**

### **Problems to Solve**

- **Lack of Pre-Approval Mechanisms**: Currently, transaction creators must manually assign approvers for every transaction. This is inefficient and prone to error, especially when specific transactions on critical entities (like treasury accounts) always require the same set of approvers. Also there is no way to enforce a mandatory approval step before a transaction is shared with the final signers.
- **Premature Transaction Visibility**: Signers can see and potentially sign a transaction before it has been reviewed and approved by the designated approvers. This creates a risk of unauthorized or incorrect transactions being executed, defeating the purpose of a sequential review process.
- **Ambiguous Transaction Status**: The user interface does not clearly distinguish between transactions that are pending approval and those that are fully approved and ready for final signatures. This ambiguity can lead to confusion and delays, as both approvers and signers may not know when it is appropriate to act.

### **Feature Summary**

This initiative introduces a multi-faceted update to the approver feature including:

**Approver Groups** - the ability to create and manage predefined "Approver Groups" for specific Hedera entities (e.g., accounts, files).

**Approval workflows** - When a transaction modifying a (configured) “permissioned” entity  is created, the configured  approver group will be automatically assigned.

**Conditional Transaction Visibility** - A  transaction that is assigned to an approver group will only become visible to the final signers after the required approvers have provided their approval signatures. The UI will be enhanced to clearly

items.title: Abstain Feature - MVP
items.body:
## Summary
 
Enable signers to formally abstain from signing individual transactions and group transactions directly from the transaction signing screen. Additionally, expose a backend API endpoint that allows an abstain action to be submitted programmatically on behalf of a user (identified by email) for a single transaction or a group transaction.
 
---
 
## In-App Abstain
 
Add an **Abstain** option to the transaction(s) summary list and transaction detail screens for both individual transactions and group transactions.
 
### Expected behavior
 
- The Abstain button or option is presented alongside the existing Sign button when a transaction requires the signer's signature and the signer has not yet signed or abstained.
- Selecting Abstain presents a confirmation dialog before the action is submitted.
- On confirmation, the abstain is recorded and the transaction is removed from the signer's Ready to Sign queue.
- The signer's line on the Signatures Collected section (on transaction detail screen) is updated with `A` in a separate font color to distinguish it from signed entries.
 
---
 
## Abstain API Endpoint (Back End)
 
Expose an API endpoint that allows an abstain action to be submitted programmatically on behalf of a user for a single transaction or a group transaction.
 
### Expected behavior
 
- The endpoint accepts a user identifier (email address) and a transaction identifier and records an abstain action for that user on the specified transaction.
- The endpoint supports individual and group transactions. As part of this work, an identifier for group transactions must be determined so that group transactions can be targeted via the API.
- The abstain is recorded with a timestamp and a source indicator distinguishing it as an API-submitted abstain.
- The endpoint is secured and restricted to authorized callers (admin-level access).
 
### Error responses
 
The endpoint returns appropriate error responses in the following cases:
 
| Condition | Expected

items.title: Optimize WebSocket `transaction_action` messages to avoid full page refreshes
items.body:
**Description:**  
WebSocket `transaction_action` messages currently trigger full page refreshes on the client, causing poor UX and unnecessary re-renders. The messages should instead convey **specific transaction updates** so the client can refresh only affected components or directly use the provided data.  

This requires coordinated backend and frontend changes:  
- **Backend**: Send full transaction data + target only relevant parties  
- **Frontend**: Subscribe pages to proper channels + partial updates (or centralized store)  

**Current behavior:**  
```
WebSocket: { action: "transaction_action" }
Client: → Full page refresh
```

**Desired behavior:**  
```
WebSocket: { 
  action: "transaction_updated", 
  transactions: [
         { id: "xyz", status: "signed", ... },
  ]
}
Client: → Update specific transaction row/card only
```

**Backend changes needed:**  
- [ ] Modify `transaction_action` payload to include full transaction data  
- [ ] Send to only relevant WebSocket channels (per key/user/org) instead of broadcast  
- [ ] Ensure transaction data excludes sensitive fields  

**Frontend changes needed (choose one approach):**  
**Option A: Per-page subscriptions**  
- [ ] Subscribe each transaction-related page to proper WebSocket channel(s)  
- [ ] Parse incoming transaction data and update only affected components  
- [ ] Remove full page refresh logic  

**Option B: Centralized store (recommended)**  
- [ ] Create global transaction store that listens to WebSocket updates  
- [ ] All pages/components read from centralized store  
- [ ] Store handles optimistic updates, cache invalidation, etc.  

**Acceptance criteria:**  
- WebSocket `transaction_action` messages include full transaction data 
- Backend sends updates only to relevant parties (not broadcast to all clients)  
- Frontend no longer does full page refreshes on transaction updates  
- Specific transaction components update correctly with new data  
- (Option B only) Centralized store exist

items.title: Add manual resync control to reduce unnecessary signature cache refreshes
items.body:
**Description:**  
When a transaction is created and sent to the backend, the backend determines which keys are required to sign the transaction and caches this key list in the database. The database periodically refreshes this cached data and prunes entries that are no longer needed, even though updates that actually change required signatures are very rare. Currently, this refresh happens frequently (on the order of every few seconds), which is unnecessary in most cases but still required so that, in rare scenarios where an account update changes the required signatures, users are not stuck waiting for the next automatic refresh.

To balance performance with responsiveness, a manual “Resync” option should be provided so that admins or authorized users can explicitly trigger a refresh of the cached required-signature data when needed. This would allow lowering the automatic refresh frequency while preserving a good user experience in those rare edge cases.

**Current Behavior:**  
- Backend computes and caches the required signing keys for each transaction when it is created and shared to the backend.
- A background process frequently:
  - Refreshes cached data for required signatures.  
  - Prunes cached entries that are no longer needed.
- In rare cases where an account update changes which keys are required to sign a transaction, users must wait until the next scheduled refresh for the cache to reflect the change.
- There is no user- or admin-facing control to force an immediate refresh of the required-signature cache for a transaction or organization.

**Problem:**  
- Automatic refreshes are happening much more often than needed, consuming resources while almost never updating any data.
- Simply reducing the refresh frequency would cause poor UX in the rare cases where a change does matter, because users would have to wait for the next scheduled update.
- Admins and users lack a mechanism to manually resync the required-signature information when they know an

items.title: Show client version and update availability for users in contact list
items.body:
Admin feedback: The old tool would output a file indicating when a user had upgraded to a newer version of the tool. This functionality is still desired for the new tool.

**Description**  
Admins would like visibility into which client versions users are running so they can identify who needs to update. When a user logs into an org, the client version is already tracked and stored by the backend. The contact list and user details views should surface this information and indicate when an update is available.

**Requirements**

- Enhance the contact list so that:  
  - Each user entry shows an “Update available” indicator if any of that user’s registered clients are on a version that can be upgraded based on the org’s current minimum/target version logic.  
  - Users with no available updates continue to display as they do today (no extra indicator).

- Enhance the user details view so that:  
  - It lists all clients registered for that user (e.g., by device name, platform, or client ID, depending on what the backend provides).  
  - For each client, display the last known client version that registered with the backend.  
  - If applicable, also show whether an update is available for each client (e.g., via a badge or status text next to the version).

- General behavior:  
  - Version and “update available” status should refresh based on the latest data sent from clients on login or periodic check‑ins.  
  - The feature should work across orgs, respecting any org‑specific version policies (minimum required, recommended, etc.).  

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Admins can see an “Update available” indicator in the contact list for any user with at least one outdated client.  
- Selecting a user from the contact list shows a details page listing all known clients and their last registered versions.  
- For a test setup with mixed client versions (some current, some outdated), the indicators and version information are accurate and consistent with the backend data.  
-

items.title: Hide "external" badge from non-admin users due to confusion
items.body:
**Description:**  
The "external" badge (indicating keys required that are not available to the backend) is causing confusion for non-admin users based on initial feedback. Non-admin users don't have the context to understand what it means or what action to take.  

**Current behavior:**  
- All users see the "external" badge on transactions requiring external keys  
- Non-admins report confusion about its meaning and next steps  

**Proposed change:**  
- Hide the "external" badge from non-admin users entirely  
- Admins continue to see it for full transparency/debugging  

**Scope:**  
- Add user role/permission check before rendering "external" badge  
- Non-admins: badge hidden (no visual indicator)  
- Admins: badge shown as before  

**Acceptance criteria:**  
- [ ] Non-admin users no longer see "external" badges  
- [ ] Admin users continue to see "external" badges unchanged  
- [ ] Badge rendering gated by clear permission check  
- [ ] Gather additional user feedback on badge UX before reconsidering visibility for non-admins  

**Follow-up:**  
Revisit badge visibility/UX after collecting more user feedback from both admin and non-admin users.

items.title: Handle mandatory update refusal by disconnecting from organization only (not quitting app)
items.body:
## Description
When a user is prompted with a **mandatory update modal**, refusing the upgrade should **not** quit or close the app entirely.  
Instead, the user should remain in the app but be **disconnected from the specific organization** that required the upgrade.

The goal is to improve usability by allowing the user to continue using other connected organizations while clearly communicating that one organization requires an upgrade before reconnection.

### Expected Behavior
- If a mandatory upgrade prompt appears, and the user selects **“Refuse”** or **“Not now”**:
  - The app does **not** quit.
  - The user’s connection to the specific organization is **set to “disconnected.”**
  - The user is informed that they cannot reconnect to that organization until they upgrade.
  - The modal provides clear messaging about how to upgrade when ready.

- When the user later attempts to reconnect (manually or automatically):
  - The app should show the same mandatory update prompt again if the organization still requires an upgrade.

### Settings Enhancements
To support this change, update the **Settings → Organizations** view with:
- A **connection status indicator** for each organization:
  - e.g., `Connected`, `Live`, `Disconnected`, or similar. (Live would indicate that the websocket is still connected)
- A **Reconnect** button that attempts to re-establish the connection along with a **Disconnect** button
- Or even better, implemented as a **toggle switch** (`Connected` / `Disconnected`) rather than two separate buttons.

## Requirements
- Modify the mandatory update modal flow to:
  - Disconnect only from the org requiring the upgrade if refused.
  - Keep the rest of the app and other orgs active.
- Update settings UI:
  - Show each organization’s connection state.
  - Include reconnect and disconnect controls.
- On reconnect attempt:
  - Retry connection; if upgrade is still required, re-show the mandatory update modal.
- All disconnect actions disconnects the web

items.title: feat: revise email notification system to reduce per transaction noise

**Description**:
This pull request introduces support for new transaction notification types (`FAILED` and `REJECTED`) and implements robust group email batching for transaction status updates. The batching logic ensures that users receive a single consolidated email per group when all relevant transactions reach the same lifecycle stage, reducing email noise and improving clarity. Additionally, the email sending infrastructure is made more resilient by ensuring all queued emails are flushed on service shutdown, and several configuration and test improvements are included.

items.title: fix: organization transaction life cycle tests
items.body:
Summary

  - Re-enables the two previously-skipped tests in organizationTransactionLifecycleTests.test.ts ("Ready for Execution" and "History").
  - Replaces the API-based co-signing helper (signTransactionByAllUsersViaApi) with the original UI flow — log out, log in as each org user via logInAndSignTransactionByAllUsers, then log back in as the first user.

  Why

  After #2783 these two tests were temporarily skipped because the API-signing path was unstable for the AccountUpdate against a complex-key account: the transaction would stall in "Collecting Signatures" and never advance to "Ready
  for Execution"/"History", so the stage assertions timed out.

  Suspected front-end bug (why we stay on the UI path for now)

  We believe the API-signing failure is not in the automation helper but in the desktop app's handling of API-uploaded signatures for transactions whose key tree contains a nested threshold (the fixture key is 1 OF (user0_key, 2 OF 2
   (user1_key, user2_key))):

  - When users[1] and users[2] upload their signatures via POST /transactions/:id/signatures, the backend stores both TransactionSigner rows, but the FE's signature-progress evaluator appears to not recognize that the inner 2 OF 2
  threshold is satisfied — so the UI never marks the root threshold as satisfied and the executor never picks the transaction up.
  - The same multi-sig key works correctly when each user signs through the Ready to Sign UI flow: each click pushes the signature into the SDK transaction bytes via the same code path the executor reads, and the threshold evaluator
  agrees the key is satisfied. That's why the UI flow is reliable while the API flow is not.
  - Likely culprits to investigate in a follow-up:
    - the signature-aggregation/threshold-evaluation logic in the renderer (front-end/src/renderer/.../transactionSignatureProgress* or equivalent) that maps stored signer rows back onto the complex-key tree.
    - whether sdkTransaction.addSignat

items.title: feat: parallel execution shared job
items.body:
**Description**:
This PR replaces the single shared E2E CI job with a remote shared-environment model so `@local-transactions`, `@organization-basic`, and `@organization-advanced` can run as separate rerunnable jobs while still provisioning `solo` and the back end only once per workflow run.
* Add remote shared E2E lifecycle scripts for provision, status, SSH tunnel setup, diagnostics collection, and cleanup
* Split front-end automation CI into `test-local-basic`, `provision-shared-e2e-env`, `shared-e2e-test`, and `cleanup-shared-e2e-env`
* Run `@local-transactions`, `@organization-basic`, and `@organization-advanced` as separate shared-env matrix jobs
* Keep suite jobs pointed at `localhost` by forwarding the remote organization API, Postgres, mirror node, and localnet node ports over SSH
* Allow suite job reruns to reprovision the remote shared environment automatically if it was already cleaned up
* Upload shared environment metadata and remote diagnostics as workflow artifacts
* Add a dedicated plan document for the external shared E2E environment architecture

items.title: fix: suppress in app update notification when running version meets minimum version threshold
items.body:
**Description**:
This pull request refactors and simplifies the version compatibility and upgrade flow in the frontend, with a focus on centralizing version checks, improving the handling of compatibility warnings, and enhancing the user interface for upgrade modals. The main changes remove redundant logic, delegate version checks to the appropriate components, and streamline how compatibility results are surfaced to users.

**Version check and compatibility logic refactor:**
- Removed version check and compatibility check logic from `GlobalAppProcesses.vue`, delegating these responsibilities to the `AutoLoginInOrganization` component and the version state store, reducing duplication and improving maintainability.
- Updated the test helper `setOrganizationVersionStateForTesting` to derive version status from the shape of the test data, ensuring the real computed pipeline is exercised and removing an unused test hook.

**Upgrade modal improvements:**
- Enhanced the `MandatoryUpgrade.vue` and `OptionalUpgrade.vue` modals to provide more informative and dynamic compatibility warnings, including improved messaging, summary, and warning slots, as well as better handling of multiple organizations requiring updates.
- In `MandatoryUpgrade.vue`, improved the proceed logic to disconnect all conflicting organizations before proceeding with the update, with error handling and user feedback.

**State management and UI consistency:**
- Both upgrade modals now use the centralized compatibility results from the version state store, improving consistency and reliability of compatibility information.
- The modals now respect the initial version check state to avoid showing upgrade prompts prematurely.

**Code cleanup:**
- Removed unused imports and logger instances, and eliminated dead code related to version checks and compatibility logic that is now handled elsewhere.

These changes collectively make the upgrade and compatibility experience more robust, maintaina

items.title: fix: Investigate flaky backend cache query test in CI
items.body:
Fixes: [#2576](https://github.com/hashgraph/hedera-transaction-tool/issues/2576)

**Description**:

`../../libs/common/src/sql/queries/cache.queries.spec.ts` is failing intermittently in CI, and it appears at least once on every PR. The failure output is minimal and does not provide enough context to diagnose the issue. We should start by enabling additional logging in the `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, `beforeAll`, and `afterAll` hooks to get more information about the test setup and teardown.

**Requirements**:

- Investigate the flaky failure in cache.queries.spec.ts.
- Add logging around beforeEach, afterEach, beforeAll, and afterAll to capture more context when the test fails.
- Improve test output so the root cause can be identified more easily in CI.
- Keep the test behavior unchanged aside from added diagnostics.

**Summary**:

The spec named in the issue (`cache.queries.spec.ts`) was not the cause. Two other specs in the same Notifications worker, `email.service.spec.ts` and `websocket.gateway.spec.ts`, were missing `jest.mock('ioredis')` (a pattern already adopted by `blacklist.service.spec.ts`, `scheduler.service.spec.ts`, and `redis-io.adapter.spec.ts`). As a result, every time their NestJS TestingModule instantiated EmailService / WebsocketGateway, the DebouncedNotificationBatcher constructor opened two real `ioredis` clients and issued `subscribe('__keyevent@0__:expired').` With no Redis on the CI runner, those clients burned through the default retry budget `(maxRetriesPerRequest: 20 × min(n*50, 2000)ms ≈ 10.5 seconds)` before throwing `MaxRetriesPerRequestError`. The error was attributed to whichever spec Jest considered "active" at that moment — usually `cache.queries.spec.ts`, because its Postgres testcontainer boot kept it on the clock for the full retry window.

**Root-cause fix**:

- `apps/notifications/src/email/email.service.spec.ts` — jest.mock('ioredis', ...) with a MockRedis class.
- `apps/notifications/src/websocket/websock

Expands UI automation coverage across the Hedera Transaction Tool by adding new Playwright scenarios, moving suitable UI checks into renderer component tests, refining the test infrastructure (per-describe parallel mode, JSON reporter, `.env` loading at config time, renderer test-hook escape hatch), and refreshing the release coverage matrix in `docs/test-scenarios.md`.

- **Notification preferences** (`@organization-basic`): Ready to Execute, Signature Required, Cancelled toggles persist state changes (3.6.2-4 + 12.2.1-4).

- **Creator-cancels in-progress transaction** (`@organization-advanced`, 6.3.7): asserts a transaction creator can cancel a transaction while it is still in progress.

items.title: chore(deps): bump axios from 1.15.2 to 1.16.0
items.body:
Bumps [axios](https://github.com/axios/axios) from 1.15.2 to 1.16.0.
Release notes

Sourced from axios's releases.

v1.16.0 — May 2, 2026

This release adds support for the QUERY HTTP method and a new ECONNREFUSED error constant, lands a substantial wave of HTTP, fetch, and XHR adapter bug fixes around redirects, aborts, headers, and timeouts, and welcomes 23 new contributors.

⚠️ Notable Changes

A handful of fixes in this release are either security-adjacent or change observable behaviour. Please review before upgrading:

  • Fetch adapter now enforces maxBodyLength and maxContentLength. These limits were silently ignored on the fetch adapter prior to 1.16.0 — anyone relying on them as a safety net (DoS protection, accidental large uploads) had no protection. (#10795)
  • Proxy requests now preserve user-supplied Host headers. Previously, the proxy path could overwrite a custom Host. Virtual-host-style routing through a proxy will now behave correctly. (#10822)
  • Basic auth credentials embedded in URLs are now URL-decoded. If you have percent-encoded credentials in a URL (e.g. https://user:p%40ss@host), the decoded value is what now goes on the wire. (#10825)
  • parseProtocol now strictly requires a colon in the protocol separator. Strings that loosely parsed as protocols before may no longer match. (#10729)

items.body:
Bumps [axios](https://github.com/axios/axios) from 1.15.2 to 1.16.0.
Release notes

Sourced from axios's releases.

v1.16.0 — May 2, 2026

This release adds support for the QUERY HTTP method and a new ECONNREFUSED error constant, lands a substantial wave of HTTP, fetch, and XHR adapter bug fixes around redirects, aborts, headers, and timeouts, and welcomes 23 new contributors.

⚠️ Notable Changes

A handful of fixes in this release are either security-adjacent or change observable behaviour. Please review before upgrading:

  • Fetch adapter now enforces maxBodyLength and maxContentLength. These limits were silently ignored on the fetch adapter prior to 1.16.0 — anyone relying on them as a safety net (DoS protection, accidental large uploads) had no protection. (#10795)
  • Proxy requests now preserve user-supplied Host headers. Previously, the proxy path could overwrite a custom Host. Virtual-host-style routing through a proxy will now behave correctly. (#10822)
  • Basic auth credentials embedded in URLs are now URL-decoded. If you have percent-encoded credentials in a URL (e.g. https://user:p%40ss@host), the decoded value is what now goes on the wire. (#10825)
  • parseProtocol now strictly requires a colon in the protocol separator. Strings that loosely parsed as protocols before may no longer match. (#10729)

items.title: fix: Transaction components to handle live updates
items.body:
Fixes #2305 

**Problem**
Every `TRANSACTION_ACTION` WebSocket event triggers a full data refetch on every open transaction page for every connected user, regardless of relevance. This creates unnecessary server load and poor UX. Three root causes are:
1. **Global broadcast bug** (`fan-out.service.ts`): `FanOutService.notifyClients()` ignores the per-user `NotifyClientDto[]` array entirely and calls `this.websocket.notifyClient()` — a global Socket.IO `io.emit()` broadcast to ALL connected clients. The user IDs from the DTOs are discarded.
2. **Empty payload:** The event carries `content: ''` — zero information about what changed, which transaction, or what kind of change occurred.
3. **Blind refetch on frontend**: Every component subscribing to `TRANSACTION_ACTION` calls its full fetch function on every event with no filtering. The `useWebsocketSubscription` composable silently drops the payload (callback signature was `() => void`).

**Result:** If user A signs a transaction, ALL connected users on ALL transaction pages refetch ALL their data, with loading spinners flashing, even if the change is irrelevant to them.

---

**Solution:** Targeted Refetch with Metadata-Based Filtering
Enrich the WebSocket event with transaction/group IDs and event type, route it per-user only, and let each frontend page check relevance before refetching. This eliminates the "everyone refetches everything" problem while maintaining full backward compatibility.

Edge Cases Handled:
 - **Reconnection**: `useWebsocketSubscription` re-subscribes after reconnect via `wsStore.$onAction`. Missing events during disconnection are caught on the next relevant action.
 - **Batcher merging**: Multiple events within 500ms for same user merge content arrays. Parser unions all IDs across array items.
 - **Transaction not loaded**: If no matching IDs in current view → event is silently ignored (no refetch, no error).
 - **Pre-load state**: If a page hasn't finished its initial fetch

items.title: fix: Add logging framework & format to frontend
items.body:
## Add structured logging framework to the frontend

Fixes #2414 

### Problem

The frontend does not log anything. When users encounter problems there is no diagnostic trail they can share with developers, and developers cannot reproduce issues without guesswork. Every `console.log` scattered across the codebase is silently swallowed in production builds, so errors in websocket connections, transaction signing, database migrations, IPC calls, and version checks vanish without a trace.

### Why `electron-log` instead of `log4js`

The issue suggested evaluating `log4js` first. After investigation, `electron-log` was chosen because:

1. **Already available** — `electron-log` is a dependency of `electron-updater` (which the app already uses for auto-updates), so it introduces zero additional package overhead.
2. **Electron-native** — It understands Electron's process model out of the box (main, renderer, preload), handles `app.getPath('userData')` automatically, and integrates with Electron's crash reporter.
3. **Built-in rotation** — File rotation with configurable max size and archive count is included without additional plugins.
4. **Simpler IPC** — Renderer logs can be forwarded to the main process file transport through the existing preload bridge pattern, without needing a custom transport adapter that `log4js` would require in an Electron context.

### Solution

A structured JSON logging framework that writes to a single rotating log file with automatic sensitive-data sanitization.

**Architecture:**
- **Shared layer** (`shared/utils/logging.ts`) — log level types, argument normalization, key-based redaction, pattern scrubbing (JWT, Bearer, PEM), payload summarization, circular-reference detection, and depth-limited serialization.
- **Main process** (`main/modules/logger.ts`) — configures `electron-log` with a JSON file transport, log rotation (5 MB × 5 archives), console patching, and unhandled error capture. Exposes `createLogger(compone

items.title: feat: improve NATS connection resilience and error handling
items.body:
Fixes #2448


| Step | File | Before | After |
|------|------|--------|-------|
| **1. Connection state tracking** | `nats-jetstream.service.ts` | `connect()` blocks forever if NATS is down (`maxReconnectAttempts: -1` with no timeout). No way to check if NATS is connected. `getJetStream()` and `getManager()` always return non-null (assumes connection succeeded). | `connectWithTimeout()` races connection against a 30s timeout. If timeout wins, app continues booting in degraded mode. Added `connected` boolean, `isConnected()` method, `getConnection()` method. Return types are now `| null`. `monitorConnectionStatus()` updates `connected` flag on disconnect/reconnect events. |
| **2. Non-blocking startup** | `nats.module.ts` | Factory does `await service.onModuleInit()` — if NATS is unreachable, NestJS never finishes booting. No HTTP endpoints, no health checks, nothing works. | Factory still awaits `onModuleInit()`, but that now returns after 30s max (due to timeout in step 1). If NATS is down, logs a warning about degraded mode. The app boots and serves HTTP. |
| **3. Stream initializer resilience** | `nats-stream-initializer.service.ts` | Runs once on init with a `setTimeout(100)` hack. If NATS is down, throws error → can crash the service. No retry. | Removed the `setTimeout` hack. Retries up to 5 times with increasing delay (2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s). Checks `isConnected()` before attempting. If all retries fail, logs error but **doesn't crash** (streams likely exist from previous boot). |
| **4. Publisher connection awareness** | `nats.publisher.ts` | Always tries to publish. If NATS is down, catches error, logs at `error` level, returns `{ success: false }`. No fast-fail — waits for network timeout before failing. | Checks `isConnected()` before publish. If disconnected, returns `{ success: false }` immediately (no timeout wait). Log level changed from `error` to `warn` (NATS outages are expected operational events, not bugs). |
| **5. Emit helpers with await

items.title: fix: Ensure app is fully closed before manual install/upgrade on macOS
items.body: Fixes #2284 **PROBLEM** When a user runs a .pkg installer while the app is already open (even just minimized or sitting in the dock with no visible windows), macOS silently replaces the app bundle files under the live Electron process. On the next launch this leaves stale V8 code caches and corrupted resource references, causing the app to show the old version or a blank/broken screen. DMG installs are not affected — Finder natively blocks replacing an in-use .app. **SOLUTION** This PR adds PKG installer scripts and a single-instance lock to prevent this: - Add `preinstall` script that detects a running app, shows a native macOS dialog offering to close it (with graceful quit force-quit fallback), and silently force-quits in headless/MDM environments - Add `postinstall` script that clears stale Electron/Chromium caches (GPUCache/, Code Cache/, Cache/) for all users without touching user data - Wire scripts into the PKG build via `electron-builder.yml` - Add single-instance lock (app.requestSingleInstanceLock()) so duplicate Electron processes cannot run simultaneously - a second launch focuses the existing window instead Notes for reviewer: - The `preinstall` script handles three paths: interactive GUI (shows dialog), headless/MDM (sudo installer -pkg), and error fallback (silently force-quits) - The `postinstall` cache cleanup covers both the root app-support directory and the Partitions/persist:main/ session partition - Function definitions in `index.ts` were moved above the if/else block to avoid hoisting issues in the test environment Checklist - Documented with code comments - Tested manually
